title: Spinach Mushroom Salad
description: A refreshing and savory spinach and mushroom salad, enhanced with hard-boiled eggs, crisp bacon, and a tangy dressing. Perfect as a light meal or a flavorful side dish.
instructions:
1. Remove stems from 300 g fresh spinach, tear into bite size pieces and place in a large bowl.
2. In a small pan, fry 4 slices bacon until crisp, then crumble it into the bowl.
3. Hard boil 3 eggs, peel, slice, and add them to the bowl along with 200 g sliced fresh mushrooms and 3 strips chopped green onions.
4. In a separate jar, combine 60 ml vegetable oil, 30 ml vinegar, 10 g sugar, 5 g salt, 5 g dry mustard, and 2 g pepper.
5. Cover the jar tightly and shake vigorously to emulsify the dressing.
6. Pour the dressing over the salad mixture and toss just before serving.

title: Hungarian Stuffed Cabbage - Mom'S Way
description: Hearty Hungarian stuffed cabbage rolls, filled with a mixture of ground beef and pork, barley, and spices, simmered in a rich tomato and sauerkraut sauce. A comforting and traditional meal.
instructions:
1. Core 1 head cabbage and place in boiling water; remove leaves as they wilt, then drain and trim the center veins.
2. In a pan, melt 2 tbsp bacon fat and sauté 1 chopped onion until soft.
3. In a large bowl, mix 500 g ground beef, 500 g ground pork, 100 g barley, 3 minced cloves fresh garlic, 2 tsp paprika, salt, and black pepper.
4. Place a portion of the meat mixture onto each cabbage leaf, roll tightly, and tuck in the ends.
5. In a large pot, layer half of the 500 g sauerkraut, then place the cabbage rolls on top, followed by 1 chopped green pepper and 400 g chopped tomatoes.
6. Cover with the remaining sauerkraut and add enough of the 200 ml water to reach the top layer.
7. Simmer on low heat for 90 minutes.
8. Whisk 2 tbsp flour with a small amount of cold water and stir into the pot liquid to thicken before serving.

title: Cream Cheese Blintzes
description: Sweet and creamy blintzes made with flattened white bread, filled with a rich cream cheese mixture, then dipped in butter and rolled in cinnamon sugar. A delightful dessert or breakfast treat.
instructions:
1. Remove crusts from the 2 loaves white sandwich bread and roll each slice flat with a rolling pin.
2. In a medium bowl, mix 450 g cream cheese, 100 g sugar, and 2 egg yolks together until smooth.
3. Spread a thin layer of the cheese mixture evenly over each bread slice and roll up tightly lengthwise.
4. Melt 120 g butter in a shallow dish. In a separate bowl, combine 150 g sugar and 10 g cinnamon.
5. Dip each roll into the melted butter and then roll in the cinnamon sugar mixture to coat.
6. Place the rolls on a tray and cool in the fridge for 20 minutes, then cut each roll into thirds.
7. Arrange on a baking sheet and bake at 350°F (175°C) for 20 minutes or until bubbly and golden brown.

title: All-In-One Meat Loaf Dinner(This Dish Is Similar In Flavor To Stuffed Cabbage.)
description: A comforting all-in-one meatloaf dinner, featuring a savory lean ground beef loaf baked with cabbage, onions, and tomatoes, reminiscent of stuffed cabbage flavors. Garnished with Swiss cheese and fresh parsley.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Chop 1 small head cabbage and 1 medium onion. In a 14x11x3-inch baking pan, combine 20 ml vegetable oil, the cabbage, the onion, 400 g canned tomatoes, 1 tbsp sugar, and 1 tsp salt. Mix well, cover with foil, and bake for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. While cabbage bakes, cook 150 g long grain rice in 250 ml beef broth until tender. Cool slightly.
4. In a large bowl, mix 700 g lean ground beef, 2 eggs, 100 g bread crumbs, 1 tsp thyme, 3 chopped scallions, and the cooked rice mixture.
5. Remove the pan from oven. Shape the beef mixture into a loaf or several small loaves and place on top of the cabbage mixture.
6. Cover and bake for another 35 minutes.
7. Remove foil, sprinkle with 150 g grated Swiss cheese and 30 g chopped parsley. Bake uncovered for 5-10 minutes until cheese is melted and meat is cooked through.

title: Pierogie Casserole
description: A simple and comforting pierogie casserole made with boiled noodles, potatoes, and onions. This dish offers a quick and easy way to enjoy pierogi-inspired flavors.
instructions:
1. Peel and boil 1 kg potatoes in salted water until soft, then mash and set aside.
2. Boil 500 g noodles in a separate pot according to package directions, then drain.
3. Finely dice 2 onions and sauté in a pan until golden brown.
4. Combine the cooked noodles, mashed potatoes, and sautéed onions in a large baking dish.
5. Bake at 175°C for 20 minutes until heated through.

title: Blintz Bubble Ring
description: An innovative blintz bubble ring, featuring cream cheese balls wrapped in refrigerator biscuits, rolled in cinnamon sugar, and baked with butter and pecans. A sweet and indulgent treat perfect for any occasion.
instructions:
1. Cut 225 g cream cheese into 20 equal pieces and roll each into a small ball.
2. Roll each of the 400 g refrigerator biscuits out into a 3-inch circle.
3. Combine 1 tbsp cinnamon with preferred sweetener if desired (or use plain).
4. Place 1 cheese ball and a sprinkle of the cinnamon into the center of each biscuit circle; pinch the dough tightly to seal.
5. Melt 50 g butter and pour it into the bottom of a 5-cup ring mold.
6. Sprinkle 25 g of chopped pecans and half of the remaining cinnamon into the mold.
7. Place half of the dough rolls into the mold with the seam side facing up.
8. Repeat the layers with the remaining 25 g pecans, cinnamon, and dough rolls.
9. Bake at 350° for 20 minutes or until golden brown.
10. Cool in the pan for 5 minutes, then invert onto a serving plate.

title: Pasta Pierogi
description: A straightforward pasta pierogi dish combining boiled potatoes and noodles with sautéed onions, then topped with melted American cheese. A comforting and easy-to-prepare meal.
instructions:
1. Peel 4 large potatoes, boil until soft, then drain and mash.
2. Cook 500 g noodles according to package directions, then drain.
3. Finely chop 1 medium onion and saute in a pan until tender and translucent.
4. Add the mashed potatoes and cooked noodles to the pan with the onion, stirring well until thoroughly coated.
5. Layer 200 g American cheese over the top of the mixture.
6. Cover until the cheese is melted, then serve immediately.

title: Russian Dressing For 50
description: A classic Russian dressing suitable for large gatherings, featuring a blend of mayonnaise, eggs, peppers, pimento, relish, chili sauce, lemon, onion, and garlic. Perfect for salads or as a dip.
instructions:
1. Hard-boil 12 eggs, cool, peel, and finely chop.
2. Finely mince 2 large green pepper, 100 g pimento, and 1 onion.
3. Mince 2 cloves garlic and squeeze the juice from 2 lemon.
4. In a large mixing bowl, combine 2.5 kg mayonnaise and 500 ml chili sauce until smooth.
5. Fold in the chopped 12 eggs, minced 2 large green pepper, 100 g pimento, 400 g sweet pickle relish, minced 1 onion, 2 cloves garlic, and the juice of 2 lemon.
6. Mix thoroughly until well combined and store in the refrigerator until ready to serve.

title: Houston Baked Beans
description: Hearty Houston baked beans, enriched with a mix of pork and kidney beans, brown sugar, and a crispy bacon topping. A perfect side dish for any barbecue or comforting meal.
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 175°C (350°F).
2. Chop 150 g bacon and 500 g pork into bite-sized pieces.
3. In a large oven-safe skillet or pot, sauté the 150 g bacon until crisp.
4. Add the 500 g pork to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides.
5. Stir in 400 g beans, 400 g kidney beans, and 100 g brown sugar until well combined.
6. Top the mixture with 200 g shredded Cheddar cheese.
7. Bake uncovered for 60 minutes until the sauce is bubbly and the cheese is melted.

title: Edna'S Jam Cake
description: A delightful jam cake featuring four layers of moist cake, infused with spices like nutmeg, allspice, cinnamon, and cloves, and enriched with raisins, coconut, and blackberry jam. A truly indulgent dessert.
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 175°C (350°F) and grease four cake pans.
2. Cream 200 g butter with 400 g sugar in a large bowl until light and fluffy.
3. Add 3 eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
4. Stir in 300 ml blackberry jam until fully integrated into the mixture.
5. Sift together 400 g flour with 1 tsp nutmeg, 1 tsp allspice, 1 tsp cloves, and 2 tsp cinnamon.
6. Add the sifted dry ingredients to the butter mixture alternately with 240 ml sweet milk, beginning and ending with the flour.
7. Fold in 150 g chopped nuts, 200 g raisins, and 100 g coconut until evenly distributed.
8. Divide the batter equally between the four prepared cake pans.
9. Bake for approximately 25 to 30 minutes until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
10. Cool the cake layers in the pans for 10 minutes before transferring to wire racks.

title: Butterscotch Cream Cake
description: A decadent butterscotch cream cake, prepared from a mix but elevated with milk, eggs, butterscotch dessert mix, and vanilla. This rich cake promises a delightful butterscotch flavor perfect for any celebration.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven according to cake package instructions and grease three 9-inch layer pans.
2. In a large bowl, combine 1 package cake layer mix, 250 ml milk, 3 eggs, 100 g sugar, 150 g butterscotch dessert mix, and 1 teaspoon vanilla.
3. Mix well and divide the batter evenly into the prepared pans.
4. Bake according to package directions, then cool in pans for 10 minutes before removing to cool completely.
5. In a saucepan, combine 50 g sugar, 100 g butterscotch dessert mix, 120 ml milk, 60 ml water, 115 g butter, and 1 beaten egg.
6. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until thick; remove from heat and fold in 100 g flaked coconut, 100 g chopped pecans, and 1 teaspoon caramel flavor.
7. In a separate bowl, beat 500 ml whipping cream with 100 g brown sugar and 1 teaspoon vanilla extract until stiff peaks form.
8. Spread the coconut-pecan filling between the cake layers and frost the exterior with the whipped cream mixture.

title: Mini-Blinies
description: Mini-blinies crafted from flattened white bread, filled with a sweet cream cheese and egg yolk mixture, then rolled, dipped in butter, and coated in cinnamon sugar. A charming and easy alternative to traditional blini.
instructions:
1. Trim the crusts from 12 slices white bread and flatten each slice firmly with a rolling pin.
2. In a small bowl, mix 225 g cream cheese, 2 egg yolks, and 50 g sugar until smooth.
3. Spread a portion of the cream cheese mixture onto each bread slice and roll them up tightly like a jelly roll.
4. Melt 115 g butter in one bowl, and in another bowl, combine the remaining 50 g sugar with 5 g cinnamon.
5. Dip each bread roll into the melted butter, then roll in the cinnamon-sugar mixture to coat evenly.
6. Place the prepared rolls on a cookie sheet and freeze until firm before serving or storing.

title: Pickled Polish Sausage
description: Zesty pickled Polish sausage, cut into pieces and simmered before being submerged in a tangy brine of vinegar, sugar, salt, peppercorns, allspice, and onion. A flavorful and hearty appetizer or snack.
instructions:
1. Cut 500 g sausage into 2 to 3-inch pieces.
2. Place the sausage in a pot, simmer for 1 hour, then drain and rinse with cold water.
3. In a separate saucepan, combine 500 ml white vinegar, 250 ml water, 50 g sugar, 15 g salt, 10 peppercorn, and 5 allspice.
4. Bring the liquid mixture to a boil and simmer for 10 minutes.
5. Remove the liquid from heat and let it cool completely.
6. Chop 1 onion and place it into a large glass jar along with the cooled sausage pieces.
7. Pour the cooled vinegar mixture over the sausage and onion.
8. Seal the jar and keep in the refrigerator.

title: Casey'S Delicious Honey Rye Bread
description: Casey's delicious honey rye bread, featuring a comforting blend of milk, honey, salt, caraway seeds, rye flour, and wheat flour. This homemade bread offers a rich flavor and satisfying texture, perfect for any meal.
instructions:
1. In a saucepan, mix together 350 ml milk, 85 g honey, 10 g salt, and 1 tbsp caraway seed; heat to dissolve then cool to room temperature.
2. When cooled, add 7 g granular yeast to the liquid mixture.
3. Add 250 g light rye flour and 125 g of the unbleached wheat flour; beat thoroughly.
4. Add the remaining 325 g unbleached wheat flour and mix to make a stiff dough.
5. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured board or counter.
6. Knead the dough until it is smooth and elastic.
7. Form the dough into a ball and place it into a greased plastic bowl.
8. Turn the ball around in the bowl evenly to coat the surface, then position it so the coated bottom faces up.
9. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and store in a warm place (approx. 30°C/85°F) until doubled in size, about 45 minutes.
10. Remove the dough onto a floured surface and divide into two equal pieces without punching the air out.
11. Shape into two loaves and place in greased 9 x 5 x 3-inch pans.
12. Cover with plastic wrap and allow to rise again before baking.

title: Russian Spice Tea
description: A quick and easy Russian spice tea mix, combining powdered orange drink, instant tea, sugar, cinnamon, and cloves. Simply add to hot or cold water for a a warming and aromatic beverage.
instructions:
1. In a large dry bowl, combine 500 g powdered orange drink, 150 g instant tea, 200 g sugar, 100 g sugar, 10 g cinnamon, and 5 g cloves.
2. Mix all ingredients thoroughly until the color and texture are uniform.
3. Transfer the mixture into a clean, airtight jar for storage.
4. To serve, stir 2 to 3 teaspoons of the mixture into 1 cup (250 ml) of hot or cold water.
5. For large batches, mix 1 cup (approx 150-200 g) of the powder with 10 cups (2.5 l) of water.

title: Lazy Pierogi
description: A comforting 'lazy pierogi' dish, combining egg noodles and cooked cabbage with browned hamburger, flavored with lemon juice, onions, and salt pork. A hearty and flavorful one-pan meal.
instructions:
1. Boil 500 g egg noodles in salted water until tender, then drain and set aside.
2. Core and quarter 1 medium cabbage; cook in boiling water with 1 tablespoon lemon juice for 25 minutes until done.
3. Drain the cooked cabbage and dice it finely.
4. Brown 500 g hamburger in a pan, drain the fat, and set the meat aside in a bowl.
5. In the same pan over low heat, cook 100 g salt pork until most of the drippings are rendered.
6. Remove and discard the pork rind.
7. Add 115 g butter to the pork drippings in the pan.
8. Add 2 sliced onions and the diced cabbage to the pan; saute until tender and lightly browned.
9. Season the mixture with salt to taste.
10. Stir the cooked hamburger and the boiled egg noodles into the pan mixture, heating through until well combined.

title: Russian Tea Balls
description: Delicate Russian tea balls, made with margarine, powdered sugar, vanilla, flour, and nuts, then baked until light brown and rolled in powdered sugar. These melt-in-your-mouth cookies are perfect for holidays or tea time.
instructions:
1. In a large bowl, cream 225 g margarine and 60 g of the powdered sugar until smooth.
2. Stir in 5 ml vanilla, then gradually mix in 280 g flour, 1 g salt, and 120 g finely chopped nuts until a dough forms.
3. Cover and refrigerate the dough for 1 hour to firm up.
4. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Roll the dough into small balls about 2.5 cm in diameter.
5. Place balls on an ungreased baking sheet and bake for 10-12 minutes or until light brown.
6. Let cool for 2 minutes, then roll the warm cookies in the remaining 60 g powdered sugar.
7. Allow to cool completely and roll in the powdered sugar a second time before serving.

title: Mushroom Caviar
description: Savory mushroom caviar, prepared by cooking onions and mushrooms with coriander, salt, and pepper until most of the liquid evaporates. This flavorful spread is perfect served chilled with black bread.
instructions:
1. Finely chop 2 medium onions and sauté in 30 ml olive oil over medium heat until soft.
2. Clean and chop 500 g mushrooms, then add them to the pan along with 1/2 tsp coriander and 1 tsp salt.
3. Continue cooking until the mushrooms release their liquid.
4. Increase the heat to high and cook, stirring frequently, until most of the moisture has evaporated.
5. Transfer the mixture to a container and refrigerate until thoroughly chilled.
6. Adjust seasoning if necessary, then garnish with 15 g chopped parsley.
7. Serve the mushroom caviar chilled with 4 slices of black bread.

title: Layered Tostada Dip
description: A flavorful layered tostada dip, featuring a base of seasoned beans, topped with a creamy avocado mixture, olives, tomatoes, green chilies, and Monterey Jack cheese. Perfect for sharing at any gathering.
instructions:
1. In a bowl, combine 450 g beans and 1 tbsp chili powder. Mix well.
2. In a separate bowl, mash 3 avocados and mix with 50 ml salad dressing, 6 slices diced crisply cooked bacon, 1 small chopped onion, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/2 tsp pepper sauce.
3. Spread the bean mixture into the bottom of a shallow serving bowl.
4. Spread the avocado mixture evenly over the bean layer.
5. Top with 100 g sliced olives, 100 g diced green chilies, and 200 g shredded Monterey Jack cheese.
6. Serve immediately with chips.

title: Easy Garden Caesar Dip
description: This easy garden Caesar dip is a quick and creamy blend of cilantro and pepper dressing, perfect for dipping vegetables or chips. Simply combine ingredients in a food processor until smooth.
instructions:
1. Thoroughly wash and roughly chop 30 g cilantro.
2. Place the 30 g cilantro and 240 ml pepper dressing into a food processor or blender.
3. Process the mixture until completely smooth.
4. Transfer the dip into a shallow bowl or a hollowed-out bell pepper and serve immediately.

title: Latkes(Potato Pancakes)
description: Golden and crispy, these potato pancakes (latkes) are made from grated potatoes, onion, and apple, bound together with egg and flour. Serve them hot with your favorite toppings.
instructions:
1. Peel and grate 1 kg potatoes, 1 medium onion, and 1 medium apple on a coarse grater.
2. In a large bowl, beat 3 eggs lightly, then add the grated potatoes, onion, and apple and blend well.
3. Stir in 1 tsp salt, 120 g flour, and 1 tsp baking powder, mixing thoroughly to combine.
4. Heat 1 inch of oil in a skillet.
5. Drain off any excess liquid from the mixture before frying.
6. Drop the mixture by tablespoons into the hot oil.
7. Fry until both sides are well browned and crispy.
8. Serve hot with sour cream or applesauce.

title: Beet Soup Or Borsch
description: A classic beet soup (Borscht) featuring julienned beets simmered in stock, thickened with a flour and sour cream mixture. This comforting soup is vibrant and flavorful, perfect for a hearty meal.
instructions:
1. Bring 2 l stock to a simmer in a large pot.
2. Add 500 g julienne beets and 2 tbsp of the vinegar to the stock and simmer until beets are tender.
3. In a separate bowl, whisk 2 tbsp flour with a small amount of cold water to form a smooth paste, then whisk in 250 ml sour cream.
4. Temper the mixture by adding 6 or 7 ladles of the hot stock into the sour cream mixture one at a time, whisking constantly to prevent lumps.
5. Reduce the heat to ensure the soup is not boiling and stir the tempered cream mixture into the pot.
6. Season with salt to taste and add the remaining 1 tbsp vinegar if additional acidity is desired.

title: Ruben Casserole
description: This savory Ruben casserole layers sauerkraut, corned beef, Swiss cheese, and rye bread, seasoned with caraway seeds and onion. It's a comforting and hearty dish, baked until golden and bubbly.
instructions:
1. Drain 900 g sauerkraut and finely chop 1 medium onion.
2. In a 13x9 inch pan, combine the sauerkraut, chopped onion, 2 tablespoons parsley, and 1 teaspoon caraway seed.
3. Sprinkle 225 g of shredded Swiss cheese evenly over the sauerkraut mixture.
4. Layer 450 g of sliced corned beef over the cheese layer.
5. Top the beef with the remaining 225 g of Swiss cheese.
6. Cut 6 slices rye bread into cubes and toss with 50 g melted margarine.
7. Spread the bread cubes over the top of the casserole.
8. Bake at 190°C (375°F) for 35 minutes until the cheese is bubbly and bread is toasted.

title: Tortilla Soup(Servings:  8)
description: A flavorful tortilla soup with tender chicken, sautéed vegetables, and a hint of spice from cumin, chili powder, and Tabasco. It's simmered in a rich chicken broth and served over crispy tortilla chips.
instructions:
1. Dice 1 large onion, 2 fresh jalapeno peppers, 2 stalks celery, and mince 4 cloves garlic.
2. Heat 30 ml oil in a large pot and saute the onion, garlic, peppers, and celery until softened.
3. Stir in 10 g cumin, 15 g chili powder, and 5 ml Tabasco sauce.
4. Sprinkle 30 g flour over the vegetables and stir constantly for 1-2 minutes to cook the raw flour taste out.
5. Gradually pour in 1.5 l chicken broth and 400 g Ro-Tel tomatoes while stirring to ensure no lumps form.
6. Add 700 g chicken breast (cubed) and 2 sliced carrots to the pot.
7. Simmer the mixture for 20-25 minutes until the chicken and carrots are cooked through.
8. Serve hot over tortilla chips as desired.

title: Russian Chicken
description: Effortless Russian chicken baked with a sweet and savory sauce made from apricot preserves, onion soup mix, and dressing. This simple recipe yields tender, flavorful chicken perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 175°C (350°F) and arrange 1.5 kg chicken pieces in a baking dish with the skin side facing down.
2. Combine 300 g apricot preserves, 1 packet onion soup mix, and 250 ml dressing in a saucepan over medium heat.
3. Stir until the 300 g apricot preserves are completely melted, then pour the mixture evenly over the chicken.
4. Cover the dish tightly with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
5. Carefully remove the foil and turn the 1.5 kg chicken pieces over.
6. Continue baking uncovered for an additional 30 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.

title: E Z Chicken Strips
description: Quick and easy chicken strips, coated in a seasoned flour and egg batter, then cooked until golden. These tender and juicy strips are perfect for a snack or light meal.
instructions:
1. Cut 500 g chicken breasts into uniform strips.
2. In a bowl, whisk together 125 g flour, 1 tsp baking powder, and 1/2 tsp salt.
3. In a separate bowl, whisk 1 egg and 60 ml milk until well combined.
4. Gradually stir the milk and egg mixture into the dry ingredients to create a smooth batter.
5. Dip each chicken strip into the batter, ensuring they are fully coated on all sides.
6. Heat oil in a large skillet and deep fry the strips until they are golden brown and cooked through.

title: Kolbasi And Sauerkraut
description: A traditional dish combining savory kolbasi sausage with tangy sauerkraut, simmered with onions and salt pork. This comforting meal is hearty and full of robust flavors.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 160°C (325°F). Place kolbasi in a pan, cover with water, and bake for 1 hour.
2. Thoroughly rinse 900 g sauerkraut under cold water and drain well.
3. Place the drained sauerkraut in a pot with 2 whole onions and 250 g salt pork.
4. Season with 10 g salt (or to taste), add water to cover the ingredients, and simmer for 45 minutes.
5. Remove the cooked kolbasi from the oven, drain, and cut into 4-inch pieces.
6. Add the kolbasi pieces to the pot with the sauerkraut and continue cooking for 30 minutes.
7. In a small pan, melt 45 ml bacon fat and stir in 30 g flour.
8. Cook the flour mixture until it reaches a golden brown color, then stir it thoroughly into the sauerkraut and kolbasi mixture before serving.

title: Creole Chicken And Mushroom Salad
description: A vibrant Creole chicken and mushroom salad featuring tender chicken, fresh vegetables, and a zesty lemon-red wine vinaigrette. This refreshing salad is perfect for a light lunch or dinner.
instructions:
1. Cook the 300 g chicken, let it cool, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
2. Slice the 200 g fresh mushrooms, halve the 150 g cherry tomatoes, dice the 1 small zucchini and 1 large green pepper, and chop the 3 stalks scallion.
3. Arrange the leaves of 1 head leaf lettuce to line a large salad bowl.
4. In a separate bowl, toss the prepared chicken, mushrooms, tomatoes, zucchini, green pepper, and scallion together, then transfer them into the lettuce-lined bowl.
5. In a small jar or bowl, whisk together 60 ml olive oil, 30 ml red wine vinegar, 15 ml lemon juice, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp pepper, and 1/2 tsp sugar until well combined.
6. Pour the dressing over the salad and toss lightly before serving.

title: Ommy'S Russian Salad Dressing
description: This easy Russian salad dressing combines sweet and tangy flavors from catsup, sugar, vinegar, and a hint of garlic and onion. It's perfect for enhancing your favorite greens.
instructions:
1. Mince the 0.5 small onion and 1 clove garlic finely.
2. In a medium bowl, combine the minced onion and garlic with 120 ml catsup, 50 g sugar, 60 ml vinegar, 1 g salt, and 2 g paprika.
3. Whisk the mixture thoroughly until the sugar has dissolved.
4. Gradually add 60 ml oil in a slow, steady stream while beating the mixture constantly to emulsify.
5. Continue whisking until the dressing is smooth and well-combined.

title: Russian Mints
description: Decadent Russian mints made with a creamy butter-sugar base, flavored with peppermint and chocolate, layered with vanilla wafers. These delightful treats are topped with whipped cream and cherries.
instructions:
1. In a large bowl, cream together 225 g butter and 240 g powdered sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Add 2 eggs one at a time, beating thoroughly after each addition.
3. Melt 170 g chocolate and stir it into the mixture along with 1 tsp peppermint flavor and 1 tsp vanilla.
4. Crush 250 g vanilla wafers into crumbs and spoon half of them into the bottom of 20 cupcake liners.
5. Divide the chocolate mixture evenly among the liners, spooning it on top of the crumbs.
6. Cover the chocolate layer with the remaining half of the wafer crumbs.
7. Top each portion with a dab of 200 ml whipped cream and 1 maraschino cherry.
8. Place in the freezer to set for at least 1 hour before serving.

title: Sweet-Sour Kraut And Pork Chops
description: This sweet-sour kraut and pork chops dish features tender pork and potatoes baked with tangy sauerkraut and sweet pineapple. A comforting and flavorful meal, perfect for a cozy evening.
instructions:
1. Peel and slice 3 large baking potatoes and 1 medium onion.
2. In a 12 x 7 x 2-inch baking dish, combine the potato slices and onion with 60 ml water.
3. Cover the dish and bake in a 350°F (175°C) oven for 45 minutes or until the potatoes are nearly tender.
4. Season 4 pork chops with salt and pepper to taste.
5. Heat 1 tbsp cooking oil in a skillet and brown the pork chops on both sides; remove chops from the pan.
6. Drain 500 g sauerkraut and mix it with 250 g pineapple and 50 g brown sugar.
7. Spread the sauerkraut mixture over the partially cooked potatoes.
8. Place the browned pork chops on top of the sauerkraut.
9. Cover and return to the oven for 30 to 40 minutes until the pork is cooked through and potatoes are soft.

title: Melissa'S Tortellini With Vegetables
description: Fresh tortellini tossed with sautéed mushrooms, tomatoes, garlic, and onion, seasoned with soy sauce and fresh basil. A quick and delicious vegetarian dish that's full of flavor.
instructions:
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook 500 g tortellini according to package directions.
2. In a medium skillet, heat 2 tbsp olive oil over medium-high heat.
3. Sauté 1 chopped onion and 200 g sliced fresh mushrooms until the onion is tender.
4. Stir in 2 cloves of minced garlic and 10 g of chopped fresh basil.
5. Add 100 g of chopped tomatoes (pre-softened if sun-dried) and 1 tbsp soy sauce to the skillet, stirring for 2 minutes.
6. Drain the tortellini and toss with the vegetable mixture.
7. Serve immediately topped with 50 g grated cheese.

title: No Peek Brisket
description: A flavorful brisket slow-cooked on a bed of rye bread with chile sauce, beer, onions, and garlic. This tender and juicy brisket is perfect for a hearty family meal.
instructions:
1. Lay 6 slices of rye bread in the bottom of a Dutch oven to form a base.
2. Place 2 kg brisket, fat side down, on top of the bread slices.
3. Mince 4 cloves garlic and sprinkle them along with 1 tsp pepper over the brisket.
4. Slice 2 large onions and scatter them into the pan around the meat.
5. Pour 330 ml beer over the brisket.
6. Spread 350 ml chile sauce evenly over the top of the meat.
7. Cover the Dutch oven tightly and cook as directed without opening the lid.

title: Chicken - Polish Style(Kurczeta Po Polsku)
description: Chicken Polish style (Kurczeta Po Polsku) features a whole chicken seasoned and stuffed with a savory bread crumb and chicken liver mixture. It's a traditional and flavorful roasted chicken dish.
instructions:
1. Sprinkle the 1.5 kg chicken thoroughly with 1 tsp of salt and let it stand for 1 hour.
2. Finely chop 200 g chicken livers and place them in a mixing bowl.
3. Combine the chopped livers with 100 g bread crumbs, 1 egg, 1 tbsp dill weed, 0.5 tsp pepper, and the remaining 1 tsp of salt.
4. Gradually stir in approximately 80 ml milk until the mixture reaches a loose, sour cream-like consistency.
5. Fill the cavity of the chicken with the liver and crumb mixture, then truss the legs together with kitchen twine.
6. Place the chicken in a roasting pan and bake at 400°F (200°C) for approximately 45 minutes or until tender.
7. Baste the chicken frequently during roasting with 50 g melted butter.

title: Broccoli And Mushroom Salad
description: A crisp broccoli and mushroom salad with celery and onion, dressed in a tangy vinaigrette with paprika and sugar. This refreshing salad is simple to prepare and full of texture.
instructions:
1. Chop 500 g broccoli, 2 stalks chopped celery, 1 small onion, 200 g mushrooms, and 2 stalks celery into bite-sized pieces.
2. In a large bowl, mix the chopped vegetables together and let them set for 1 hour.
3. In a separate jar or bowl, whisk together 30 g sugar, 120 ml vegetable oil, 60 ml vinegar, and 5 g paprika until the sugar is dissolved.
4. Pour the oil and vinegar mixture over the vegetables and toss to coat evenly.
5. Let the salad stand for 1 additional hour before serving.

title: Peasant Rye
description: A creamy peasant rye bread dip filled with a mixture of mayonnaise, sour cream, dill, garlic, and olives. Perfect for scooping with torn rye bread, it's a delightful appetizer.
instructions:
1. Finely mince 30 g onion and chop 50 g black olives.
2. In a medium bowl, combine 250 ml mayonnaise, 250 ml sour cream, 1 tbsp parsley flakes, 1 tsp dill weed, 0.5 tsp garlic powder, 0.5 tsp salt, the minced onion, and the chopped olives. Mix until thoroughly combined.
3. Cover the mixture and chill in the refrigerator for 2 to 3 hours to allow flavors to meld.
4. Using 1 loaf rye bread, slice off the top crust and carefully hollow out the center to create a cavity, keeping the bread shell intact.
5. Cut the removed bread pieces into bite-sized cubes.
6. Fill the cavity of the bread loaf with the chilled dip mixture and serve immediately with the bread cubes for dipping.

title: Potato Pancake Appetizers
description: Crispy potato pancake appetizers featuring grated potatoes, carrots, and zucchini, served with a dollop of low-fat sour cream and fresh herbs. These savory bites are perfect for any gathering.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
2. Finely grate 500 g potatoes, 1 medium carrot, and 1 small zucchini. Wrap the vegetables in paper towels and squeeze out all excess moisture.
3. In a large bowl, whisk 1 large egg with 2 tbsp flour, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/4 tsp black pepper.
4. Fold the squeezed potatoes, carrot, and zucchini into the egg mixture and mix well.
5. Grease two nonstick baking sheets with oil. Drop the mixture by heaping spoonfuls onto the sheets and flatten into pancakes.
6. Bake for 8 to 15 minutes until the bottoms are golden brown, then flip and bake for another 5 to 10 minutes.
7. Finely chop 1 tbsp fresh basil and 1 tbsp chives, then stir them into 150 ml low-fat sour cream.
8. Serve the warm potato pancakes with the herb sour cream on the side.

title: Reuben Meatballs
description: Flavorful Reuben meatballs made with ground beef, sauerkraut, caraway seeds, and rice, baked until tender. These savory meatballs are a creative twist on a classic sandwich, perfect for a hearty meal.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 175°C (350°F).
2. In a large bowl, whisk 1 large egg, then combine with 50 g onion, 30 g bread crumbs, 15 g fresh parsley, 3 g salt, and 2 g pepper.
3. Stir in 100 g cooked rice into the mixture.
4. Crumble 500 g lean ground beef over the mixture and mix well.
5. Shape the mixture into 15 meatballs and place them in an ungreased 13 x 9 x 2 inch baking dish.
6. Bake uncovered for 15-20 minutes or until browned, then drain any excess fat.
7. Spread 400 g sauerkraut over the meatballs and sprinkle with 5 g caraway seeds.
8. Pour 300 ml condensed cream evenly over the top.
9. Cover the dish and bake for 35-45 minutes until the meat is cooked through.
10. Uncover, sprinkle with 150 g Swiss cheese, and bake until the cheese is melted.
11. Serve warm with 4 slices of Rye Bread.

title: Lazy Man'S Stuffed Cabbage
description: Hearty "Lazy Man's" cabbage rolls are deconstructed and baked in a casserole, featuring a savory filling of ground beef and rice simmered in a rich tomato sauce with tender cabbage.
instructions:
1. Chop 1 medium cabbage and place in boiling water; cook until slightly soft, then drain.
2. In a skillet, brown 500 g ground beef and 1 chopped medium onion until the onions are clear and beef is no longer pink.
3. Drain the excess fat from the meat mixture.
4. Stir 300 ml tomato soup, 400 ml tomato sauce, and 120 ml water into the meat mixture.
5. Cook 200 g rice separately using 250 ml water until done.
6. Combine the cooked rice with the meat and tomato mixture.
7. In an 11 x 15-inch baking pan, layer half of the cabbage and half of the meat-rice mixture; repeat the layers once more.
8. Cover the pan with aluminum foil and bake in a 350°F (175°C) oven for 1 hour.

title: Blintzes Or Crepes (Pancakes)
description: Delicate, thin blintzes (or crepes) are pan-fried until golden, perfect for wrapping a sweet, creamy filling of eggs, sugar, and cinnamon. A delightful treat for any time of day.
instructions:
1. In a large bowl, whisk together 3 eggs, 2 tbsp sugar, and 1/2 tsp salt.
2. Gradually whisk in 500 ml milk and 250 g flour until the batter is perfectly smooth.
3. Heat a lightly greased 8-inch skillet over medium heat and pour enough batter to coat the bottom thinly.
4. Tilt the skillet to cover the surface evenly and cook until the bottom is delicately browned, then flip and cook briefly before removing.
5. Repeat the process until all batter is used up to create the crepes.
6. For the filling preparation, mix 500 g Filling (such as cottage cheese or fruit) with 2 eggs, 50 g sugar, and 1 tsp cinnamon.
7. Place a portion of the filling onto each cooked crepe, roll or fold them as desired, and serve.

title: Thin White Sauce
description: A classic thin white sauce, made with a simple roux of butter and flour, is slowly thickened with milk and seasoned with salt. Ideal as a base for many dishes.
instructions:
1. Melt 30 g butter in a saucepan over low heat and whisk in 30 g flour to combine.
2. Gradually add 500 ml milk slowly, stirring constantly to ensure it remains smooth and lump-free.
3. Cook the mixture over hot water or low heat until the sauce becomes thick and reaches a smooth consistency.
4. Stir in 1/2 tsp salt and serve immediately.

title: Pork Chops L'Orange
description: Tender pork chops are seasoned, browned, and then baked over a bed of rice infused with orange juice and chicken stock. This creates a flavorful, aromatic main course.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 175°C (350°F) and season 4 pork chops.
2. Brown the pork chops on both sides in a skillet on the stove.
3. Grease a 9 x 13-inch baking pan and spread 200 g rice evenly across the bottom.
4. Pour 250 ml orange juice over the rice.
5. Layer the browned pork chops on top of the rice.
6. Pour 500 ml salted chicken stock over the pork chops.
7. Cover the pan tightly with foil and bake for 1 hour until the liquid is absorbed and meat is tender.

title: Borsch
description: A rich and vibrant borscht, featuring beef stock, hearty vegetables like beets and cabbage, and a savory sauteed onion base with honey and vinegar for a nuanced flavor. A true taste of tradition.
instructions:
1. Pour 2 l beef stock into a large soup kettle and add to taste salt, to taste pepper, 1 tsp thyme, 3 cloves, and 2 bay leaves.
2. Peel and shred 3 medium beets and shred 0.5 head cabbage, then add them to the kettle.
3. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 45 minutes.
4. While the soup simmers, finely chop 2 medium onions and sauté them in 2 tbsp oil until golden.
5. Remove the onions from heat and stir in 1 tbsp honey, 2 tbsp cider vinegar, 2 tbsp tomato paste, and 1 tbsp soy sauce.
6. Stir the onion mixture into the soup kettle and simmer for an additional 5-10 minutes before serving.

title: Russian Corn Bread
description: This unique "Russian Corn Bread" combines cornmeal, cream-style corn, potatoes, and cheese, baked into a flavorful and moist bread with a hint of spice from hot peppers.
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C) and grease a baking pan.
2. Peel and grate 300 g potatoes; finely chop 1 onion and 2 hot peppers.
3. In a large bowl, whisk 3 eggs with 20 g sugar and 100 ml oil.
4. Stir in 250 ml milk and 400 g cream-style corn until combined.
5. Gradually fold in 500 g meal, 300 g grated potatoes, 1 chopped onion, 2 chopped hot peppers, and 200 g pimento cheese.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 45 minutes until golden brown.

title: Texas Torte(Cheese Appetizers)
description: Cheesy and savory Texas Torte appetizers are made with eggs, flour, milk, green chilies, and two types of cheddar cheese, baked until golden and bubbly. An easy crowd-pleaser.
instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 175°C (350°F) and grease an 8x12-inch baking dish.
2. In a large bowl, whisk together 10 eggs, 60 g flour, 5 g salt, and 60 ml milk until the mixture is well beaten.
3. Stir in 150 g green chilies, 450 g Cheddar cheese, and 450 g cheese until combined.
4. Pour the mixture into the prepared baking dish.
5. Bake for 35 minutes until the center is set.
6. Cut into small squares and serve warm.

title: Bavarian Kielbasa With Noodles
description: A comforting dish of Bavarian kielbasa and onion, browned and then simmered in a creamy mushroom soup sauce, served over tender egg noodles. Quick and satisfying.
ingredients: 300 ml Campbell's condensed cream of mushroom soup
instructions:
1. Boil 250 g egg noodles according to package directions, then drain.
2. Slice 500 g kielbasa and chop 1 large onion; cook both in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat until browned.
3. Spoon off any excess fat from the skillet.
4. Add 300 ml Campbell's condensed cream of mushroom soup to the skillet and stir until smooth.
5. Gradually stir in 120 ml milk and 0.5 tsp pepper.
6. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low.
7. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
8. Stir the cooked egg noodles into the skillet.
9. Heat for an additional 2 minutes while stirring often and serve.

title: Pizza Rounds
description: Savory pizza rounds feature a mixture of browned ground beef, sausage, melted Velveeta cheese, and Italian seasonings spread on party rye bread. A fantastic easy appetizer.
instructions:
1. Brown 500 g ground beef and 500 g sausage in a large skillet over medium heat, then drain the grease.
2. Add 450 g Velveeta cheese to the Warm meat and stir until the cheese is completely melted.
3. Stir in 1 tsp oregano, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, and 1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce until thoroughly combined.
4. Spread a portion of the mixture onto each slice of 1 loaf party rye bread.
5. Place the rounds on a cookie sheet and freeze until solid.
6. Transfer the frozen rounds into freezer bags for storage.
7. Bake as many rounds as needed on a cookie sheet at 175°C (350°F) for 15 minutes.

title: Marion'S Rye Bread
description: A homemade rye bread recipe featuring rye flour, molasses, and a unique method involving boiling water to activate yeast. This yields a dense and flavorful loaf with a rustic appeal.
instructions:
1. In a large heatproof bowl, combine 500 g rye flour, 15 g salt, 50 g dark brown sugar, 60 g margarine, and 120 ml molasses.
2. Pour 1 quart of boiling water over the mixture and stir thoroughly until well combined.
3. Allow the mixture to cool until it reaches a lukewarm temperature of approximately 115°F (46°C).
4. Dissolve 1 package of dry yeast in 1/4 cup of warm water and stir this into the cooled rye mixture.
5. Gradually incorporate approximately 600-800 g of white flour into the bowl until a workable dough forms.
6. Knead the dough, place in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise until doubled in size.
7. Punch the dough down and divide into 4 equal loaves; place into pans or on a baking sheet.
8. Prick the tops of the loaves with a fork and let them rise a second time until light.
9. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 35 minutes or until the loaves sound hollow when tapped.

title: South Of The Border
description: "South of the Border" is an easy casserole layering crunchy corn chips, onions, kidney beans, olives, and a savory meat alternative with tomato and taco seasoning. A flavorful, satisfying meal.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 175°C (350°F).
2. Finely dice 1 medium onion and drain 400 g kidney beans and 100 g black olives.
3. Line the bottom of a 5-quart casserole dish with 150 g (half) of the corn chips.
4. Layer 1 medium onion, 400 g kidney beans, 100 g black olives, 450 g Vegetarian burger meat, and the remaining 150 g corn chips over the bottom layer.
5. In a small bowl, whisk together 400 ml tomato sauce and 1 packet taco seasoning mix.
6. Pour the sauce mixture evenly over the layered ingredients in the casserole.
7. Top the dish with 200 g grated sharp cheese.
8. Bake for 35 to 45 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbling.

title: Quick Chocolate Buttermilk Cake
description: A quick and easy chocolate buttermilk cake that's rich, moist, and deliciously comforting. This recipe is perfect for satisfying chocolate cravings with minimal effort.
instructions:
1. Sift together 0.5 tsp salt, 400 g sugar, and 250 g flour in a large mixing bowl.
2. In a saucepan, combine 115 g butter, 115 g shortening, 40 g cocoa, and 240 ml water; bring the mixture to a boil.
3. Remove the saucepan from the heat and allow the mixture to cool.
4. Pour the cooled cocoa mixture over the sifted dry ingredients and mix well until combined.
5. Beat 2 eggs, then add them along with 1 tsp soda and 120 ml buttermilk to the batter; mix thoroughly.
6. Pour into pans and bake at 400°F (200°C) for 20 to 25 minutes for layer cakes, or 28 to 32 minutes for a 9x13-inch pan.

title: Crab Cheese Puff
description: Delectable crab cheese puffs start with a creamy crab and cheese mixture, spread over toasted muffin halves, and then broiled until warm and golden. An irresistible appetizer.
instructions:
1. Mince the 1 clove garlic and combine it with 200 g crab meat, 100 g softened margarine, and 120 ml mayo in a bowl.
2. Add a small amount of water from the 1 glass to reach a spreadable consistency.
3. Slice 8 muffins in half and toast them under a broiler until lightly browned.
4. Spread a thin layer of the crab mixture onto each of the 16 muffin halves.
5. Place the muffins back under the broiler and cook until the topping begins to bubble and turn golden.
6. Serve the muffins immediately while hot.

title: World'S Easiest Chicken
description: The "World's Easiest Chicken" features chicken breasts baked under a rich, creamy sauce made from a blend of condensed soups, Parmesan cheese, and almonds. A simple, comforting dinner.
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 175°C (350°F).
2. Place 4 chicken breasts into a 9 x 13-inch casserole dish.
3. In a medium bowl, combine 300 ml cream of chicken soup, 300 ml cream of mushroom soup, and 300 ml cream of celery soup.
4. Pour the soup mixture evenly over the chicken breasts.
5. Sprinkle 100 g of Parmesan cheese and 50 g of almonds over the top of the mixture.
6. Bake uncovered for approximately 1 hour and 15 minutes.
7. Serve hot with a side of rice.

title: Tomato And Mushroom Salad
description: A refreshing salad combining juicy cherry tomatoes, earthy fresh mushrooms, and crisp green onions, all tossed in a tangy vinaigrette dressing. Perfect for a light meal or side dish.
instructions:
1. Halve the 250 g cherry tomatoes, thinly slice the 200 g fresh mushrooms, and chop the 30 g green onions.
2. In a large bowl, combine the prepared cherry tomatoes, mushrooms, and onions.
3. In a separate small container, whisk together 45 ml vegetable oil, 15 ml vinegar, 10 ml lemon juice, 5 ml Worcestershire sauce, 30 ml vegetable broth, and 1/2 tsp T until emulsified.
4. Pour the dressing over the vegetables and stir gently to coat all ingredients.
5. Place the salad in the refrigerator to chill for two hours before serving.

title: Borscht(Beet Soup)
description: This quick beet soup, or borscht, combines sautéed onions with beef broth, beet juice, and cabbage for a vibrant and warming meal. Best served with a dollop of sour cream and crispy saltines.
instructions:
1. Finely dice 1 medium onion. In a large pot, melt 30 g butter over medium heat and saute the onion until translucent.
2. Peel and grate 2 medium beets and shred 300 g cabbage.
3. Add 1 l beef broth, 500 ml water, 250 ml beet juice, 1 tbsp vinegar, 2 tbsp ketchup, the shredded cabbage, and the grated beets into the pot.
4. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
5. Cover the pot and simmer for approximately 10 to 15 minutes, or until the cabbage is tender.
6. Serve hot, optionally topped with a dollop of sour cream.

title: Stroganoff Bread
description: A comforting take on Stroganoff, served hot on toasted French bread, topped with melted cheese, green onions, and fresh tomatoes. Quick and satisfying, perfect for a cozy meal.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 190°C. Cut the 1 loaf French bread in half lengthwise.
2. Spread 30 g margarine on the cut sides and toast in the oven for 5 to 10 minutes until lightly browned.
3. In a large skillet, brown 500 g ground beef with 3 chopped green onions over medium heat; drain excess fat.
4. Stir 240 ml sour cream, 60 ml milk, 15 ml Worcestershire sauce, and 5 g garlic salt into the beef mixture.
5. Heat the mixture thoroughly without letting it come to a boil.
6. Spoon the beef mixture onto the toasted bread halves.
7. Top with 2 sliced tomatoes, 1 sliced green pepper, and 150 g shredded cheese.
8. Return to the oven for 5 minutes or until the cheese is melted.

title: Trail Cookies
description: These delightful trail cookies are packed with wholesome oats, rich peanut butter, and sweet chocolate chips and raisins. A perfect snack for on-the-go or a sweet treat at home.
instructions:
1. In a small bowl, whisk together 250 g flour, 1 tsp baking soda, and 1/2 tsp salt; set aside.
2. In a large bowl, beat 115 g butter, 125 g peanut butter, 100 g sugar, and 100 g brown sugar until the mixture is creamy.
3. Add 2 eggs to the creamed mixture one at a time, beating well after each addition.
4. Gradually stir the flour mixture into the wet ingredients.
5. Fold in 180 g oats, 170 g semi-sweet chocolate pieces, and 75 g raisins until evenly distributed.
6. Drop rounded spoonfuls onto baking sheets and bake at 175°C for 10-12 minutes.

title: Tangy Minute Steak
description: Effortlessly delicious, this tangy minute steak recipe features a simple marinade of onion soup, mushroom soup, and a splash of soda. Baked until tender, it's a quick fix for a tasty dinner.
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 175°C (350°F).
2. In a medium bowl, combine 300 ml onion soup, 300 ml mushroom soup, and 150 ml Dew until the mixture is uniform.
3. Arrange 4 pieces of minute steak in a single layer in a baking dish.
4. Pour the soup mixture evenly over the steaks.
5. Cover the dish and bake for approximately 1 hour or until the meat is tender and the sauce is bubbling.

title: Bran 'N Beef Stroganoff
description: A unique twist on traditional Stroganoff, featuring ground beef patties enhanced with Bran Flakes for extra texture and flavor. Simmered in a creamy sauce and served over buttered noodles, it's a comforting and hearty meal.
instructions:
1. In a large bowl, beat 1 large egg until foamy.
2. Stir in 60 ml milk, 120 g Bran Flakes, 1 tsp garlic salt, 1/4 tsp pepper, and 1/2 tsp paprika.
3. Let the mixture stand for 1 to 2 minutes.
4. Add 500 g ground beef and mix until thoroughly combined.
5. Form the mixture into 20 meatballs (about 1.5 inches each). Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 15 minutes, then drain.
6. While meatballs bake, sauté 1 finely chopped medium onion in 30 g margarine until tender.
7. Stir in 120 ml hot water and 300 ml condensed cream, then whisk in 240 ml sour cream over low heat.
8. Add the cooked meatballs to the sauce and serve over 400 g buttered noodles garnished with 2 tbsp parsley.

title: Russian Cream
description: Experience the classic elegance of Russian Cream, a delicate, chilled dessert. This smooth and creamy gelatin-based treat is subtly sweet with a hint of vanilla and a tangy touch of sour cream.
instructions:
1. In a small bowl, sprinkle 15 g gelatin over 60 ml cold water and let it bloom for 5 minutes.
2. In a saucepan over medium heat, combine 250 ml cream and 150 g sugar, stirring until the sugar is fully dissolved.
3. Add the bloomed gelatin to the warm cream mixture and stir until the gelatin has completely melted.
4. Remove the saucepan from the heat and set aside to cool until the mixture begins to thicken slightly.
5. In a separate bowl, whisk together 500 g sour cream and 5 ml vanilla until smooth.
6. Gently fold the sour cream mixture into the cooled cream mixture until well combined.
7. Pour the mixture into a ring mold and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or until firm.

title: Roast Pork With Sauerkraut
description: A savory pork roast slow-cooked with flavorful sauerkraut, tart apples, and sweet brown sugar for a delightful balance of tastes. This hearty dish is perfect for a comforting meal.
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 230°C (450°F).
2. Rub the 1.5 kg pork roast thoroughly with 10 g salt and 5 g freshly ground black pepper.
3. Place the meat in a large roasting pan and sear in the oven for 20 to 30 minutes, turning to brown all sides.
4. Remove the pan from the oven and lower the temperature to 120°C (250°F).
5. Arrange 800 g sauerkraut around the sides of the roast.
6. In a bowl, combine 2 chopped tart apples, 1 chopped onion, 50 g light brown sugar, 30 g mustard, and 5 g caraway seed.
7. Spread the apple and onion mixture over the sauerkraut and pour 250 ml white wine over everything.
8. Cover the roasting pan tightly and cook at 120°C (250°F) for approximately 4 hours until the pork is tender.
9. Add a splash of additional wine if the pan becomes dry during roasting.
10. Remove from the oven and let the roast rest for 10 minutes before slicing.

title: Sauerkraut Bake
description: A simple yet savory sauerkraut bake featuring crispy bacon, sweet onions, and ripe tomatoes, all harmoniously combined. This quick side dish offers a delightful balance of textures and flavors.
instructions:
1. In a large skillet, fry 150 g bacon over medium heat until crisp, then drain on a paper towel and crumble.
2. In the same skillet, sauté 1 finely chopped onion in the bacon fat until translucent.
3. Stir in 400 g canned tomatoes, 500 g sauerkraut, and 2 tbsp brown sugar.
4. Simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes until flavors are well combined and the liquid has reduced.
5. Stir the crumbled bacon back into the dish just before serving.

title: Russian Salad
description: A delightful and unusual 'Russian Salad' that's more dessert than savory, combining marshmallows, lime gelatin, cream cheese, and fruit. Chill until set for a unique sweet treat.
instructions:
1. In a saucepan, combine 500 g marshmallows, the juice of 1 lime, 250 g cream cheese, and 250 ml hot water.
2. Place over low heat and stir until the marshmallows and cream cheese are fully dissolved.
3. Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in 100 ml milk to cool it down.
4. Fold in 400 g chopped pineapple, 100 g chopped pecans, 2 sliced bananas, and 150 g mayonnaise until well combined.
5. Pour the mixture into molds and refrigerate for several hours until set.

title: Stuffed Green Peppers(And Stuffed Zucchini And Stuffed Cabbage Rolls)
description: Hearty stuffed peppers (or zucchini/cabbage rolls) filled with a savory mixture of sausage, rice, and herbs, simmered in a tomato-based sauce. A comforting and versatile dish for any occasion.
instructions:
1. Halve 6 green peppers lengthwise, removing stem ends, seeds, and membranes.
2. Immerse peppers in boiling water for 3 minutes, then sprinkle insides with a portion of the 1 tsp salt and invert on paper towels to drain.
3. In a skillet, cook 500 g sausage and 1 chopped onion until meat is brown and onion is tender; drain the excess fat.
4. Stir in 400 g undrained tomatoes, 150 g uncooked long grain rice, 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce, 120 ml water, 1/2 tsp dried basil, 1/2 tsp sugar, and the remaining salt and 1/2 tsp pepper.
5. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15-18 minutes until rice is tender.
6. Stir 50 g of the American cheese into the mixture.
7. Fill the peppers with the mixture and place in an 8x8 inch baking dish with any remaining filling.
8. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 15 minutes, sprinkle with the remaining 150 g American cheese, and let stand for 2 minutes before serving.

title: Cold Beet Soup-Russia(Svekolnik)
description: Svekolnik, a traditional Russian cold beet soup, is a refreshingly tangy and earthy dish. Made with chicken stock, beets, potatoes, and lemon, it's perfect for a light and flavorful meal.
instructions:
1. Peel and dice 1 onion, 3 potatoes, 3 beets, and 1 carrot. Mince 2 cloves of garlic.
2. In a large pot, combine 2 l chicken stock and 500 ml water. Add the diced onion, potatoes, beets, carrot, minced garlic, and 1 tsp salt. Bring to a boil.
3. Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
4. Stir in 1 tbsp sugar and continue cooking for 30 more minutes until all vegetables are tender.
5. Remove from heat and let the soup cool to room temperature.
6. Stir in 2 tbsp fresh squeezed lemon juice.
7. Chill the soup in the refrigerator for several hours or overnight.
8. Serve cold, garnished with chopped parsley from 1 bunch and a dollop of 200 g sour cream.

title: Grandma "Field'S" Cabbage Rolls Or Stuffed Cabbage
description: Classic cabbage rolls, or Golabki, filled with a hearty mixture of ground beef and pork, rice, and seasonings, simmered in a rich tomato sauce. A beloved comfort food, perfect for family dinners.
instructions:
1. Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook 1 large head cabbage until leaves are soft enough to peel and roll.
2. Cook 200 g rice according to package directions and set aside to cool.
3. Finely dice 2 medium onions and mince 3 cloves garlic, then sauté until softened.
4. In a large bowl, mix the cooled rice, sautéed onions, garlic, 2 eggs, 500 g ground beef, 500 g pork sausage, and 1 tsp oregano until well combined.
5. Remove the core from the cabbage, peel the leaves, and place a portion of the meat mixture in the center of each leaf; roll tightly, tucking in the ends.
6. In a large Dutch oven, spread 500 g sauerkraut on the bottom and place the cabbage rolls on top.
7. Chop 1 large green pepper and 800 g tomatoes; scatter them over the rolls along with 2 bay leaf.
8. Mix 170 g tomato paste with a little water and pour over the surface.
9. Cover and simmer on low heat or bake at 175°C for 90 to 120 minutes until the cabbage is tender and meat is cooked.

title: Dijon Chicken
description: Quick and flavorful Dijon chicken breasts, coated in a zesty sour cream and mustard mixture, then breaded and baked to golden perfection. A simple yet elegant main course.
instructions:
1. Mince 2 cloves garlic and finely chop 1 tbsp fresh parsley.
2. Season 4 chicken breasts evenly with 1/2 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp pepper, and the minced garlic.
3. In a shallow dish, whisk together 120 ml sour cream and 60 ml mustard until thoroughly combined.
4. Dip each seasoned chicken breast into the mustard mixture to coat, then dredge in 150 g Italian breadcrumbs until covered.
5. Arrange the coated chicken in a single layer in a baking pan.
6. Bake at 190°C (375°F) for 40-50 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the coating is golden brown.
7. Garnish with chopped parsley before serving.

title: Betty Ann'S Cabbage Rolls
description: Betty Ann's comforting cabbage rolls feature a savory filling of hamburger, rice, and egg, wrapped in tender cabbage leaves. Simmered in a sweet and tangy tomato sauce with sauerkraut, they're a true family favorite.
instructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix 500 g hamburger, 1 egg, and 150 g rice until well combined.
2. Add 3 tablespoons of the 400 ml tomato sauce to the meat mixture and blend thoroughly.
3. Place a spoonful of the meat mixture into each leaf of the 1 large cored cabbage; roll tightly and secure with wooden toothpicks.
4. Place the cabbage rolls on a rack inside a pressure cooker.
5. Pour the remaining tomato sauce over the rolls.
6. Whisk together 50 g brown sugar, 60 ml vinegar, and 120 ml water; pour this liquid over the rolls.
7. Secure the lid and cook for 10 minutes under pressure, or simmer in a covered pot for 90 minutes if not using a pressure cooker.
8. Once cooked, add 250 g sauerkraut to the pot and simmer for an additional 10 minutes before serving.

title: Pressure Cooker Spareribs And Sauerkraut
description: Succulent spareribs cooked to tender perfection in a pressure cooker with tangy sauerkraut and sweet onions. A hearty and flavorful meal that's surprisingly quick to prepare.
instructions:
1. Brown 1.5 kg spareribs in a frying pan with oil or under a broiler until golden.
2. Place a trivet in the pressure cooker and pour in 250 ml water.
3. Peel and slice 2 medium onions.
4. Add 900 g sauerkraut, the sliced onions, and the browned spareribs into the pressure cooker.
5. Secure the lid and cook at high pressure for 1 hour.
6. Allow pressure to release naturally, then serve.

title: Waffles "Sinless"
description: Light and fluffy 'Sinless' waffles, a guilt-free indulgence for breakfast or brunch. Made with simple ingredients, they are perfect for a quick and satisfying start to your day.
instructions:
1. In a large bowl, whisk together 250 g flour, 2 tsp baking powder, 1/2 tsp baking soda, 1/4 tsp salt, and 1 tbsp sugar.
2. Gradually pour 350 ml milk into the dry ingredients and whisk until a smooth batter forms.
3. In a separate clean bowl, beat 3 egg whites until stiff peaks form, then gently fold them into the batter.
4. Preheat a waffle iron and spray it with non-stick cooking spray.
5. Pour the batter into the hot waffle iron and cook until golden brown and crisp.

title: Crustless Quiche
description: A classic crustless quiche, featuring a savory blend of eggs, milk, and Cheddar cheese, studded with sautéed mushrooms and onions. Perfect for brunch or a light meal.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C) and grease a 9-inch pie plate.
2. Finely chop 1 small onion and slice 150 g mushrooms, then sauté them in a pan until softened.
3. In a large bowl, whisk together 350 ml milk, 125 g flour, 4 large eggs, 1 tsp oregano, 0.5 tsp salt, and 140 g of the Cheddar cheese.
4. Stir the sautéed mushrooms and onions into the batter.
5. Pour the mixture into the prepared pie plate and bake for 25 minutes.
6. Remove from oven, sprinkle the remaining 60 g of Cheddar cheese on top, and bake for an additional 5 minutes until set.

title: Polish Stuffed Cabbage
description: Traditional Polish Stuffed Cabbage (Gołąbki) with tender cabbage leaves wrapped around a savory filling of rice, onion, and spices. Simmered in a sweet tomato soup, these rolls are a comforting and hearty meal.
instructions:
1. Core 1 head cabbage and steam it hole-down in a large pot with 2 inches of water until leaves are soft.
2. Pull off 22 to 26 leaves from the steamed cabbage and set aside.
3. Cook 200 g rice and set aside to cool slightly.
4. Chop 1 large onion and saute it in 50 g butter until translucent.
5. Combine 500 g meat loaf mixture with the cooked rice and sauteed onion using your hands.
6. Mix in 2 eggs and 1 tsp salt until well incorporated.
7. Place a portion of the meat mixture in the middle of each cabbage leaf and wrap tightly envelope-style.
8. Scatter half of the 100 g raisins on the bottom of the pot.
9. Pack the cabbage rolls tightly in two rows inside the pot.
10. Sprinkle the remaining raisins over the top.
11. Pour in 300 ml tomato soup and 600 ml water (2 cans worth).
12. Cover, bring to a boil, then simmer over low heat for 1 1/2 hours.

title: Cabbage-Sausage-Beef Goulash
description: A hearty Cabbage-Sausage-Beef Goulash, combining browned meats, tender cabbage, and a zesty tomato sauce with a hint of chili powder. A warming and satisfying one-pot meal.
instructions:
1. In a large kettle, brown 500 g pork sausage, 500 g ground beef, and 1 diced large onion over medium heat.
2. Drain the excess fat thoroughly.
3. Add 400 g chopped tomatoes, 30 g tomato paste, 15 ml vinegar, 5 g chili powder, 5 g garlic powder, and 2 g red pepper to the kettle; mix well.
4. Stir in 1 shredded medium head of cabbage.
5. Cover and simmer for 20 to 25 minutes or until the cabbage is tender.

title: Chipped Beef Gravy(Fat-Free)
description: A quick and easy chipped beef gravy, made fat-free by rinsing the beef. It's a simple, savory sauce perfect for a busy morning.
instructions:
1. Tear 200 g beef into small, bite-sized pieces.
2. Place the beef in a colander and rinse thoroughly under cold water to remove excess salt.
3. In a small bowl, whisk 30 g flour with a portion of the 500 ml water to create a smooth slurry.
4. Bring the remaining water to a boil in a saucepan, add the beef pieces, and stir in the flour slurry.
5. Simmer over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the gravy thickens to your desired consistency.
6. Season with 1/2 tsp pepper and serve hot.

title: Cherry Icebox Pie
description: A delightful no-bake cherry icebox pie featuring layers of graham crackers, sweet cherries, and a creamy filling. Ideal for a refreshing dessert without turning on the oven.
instructions:
1. Line the bottom of an 8x8 dish with 100 g of whole graham crackers.
2. Drain the juice from 400 g of cherries into a separate container and set the juice aside.
3. Spread the drained 400 g of cherries evenly over the layer of graham crackers in the dish.
4. Separate 3 eggs; beat the yolks and set the whites aside for another use.
5. In a saucepan over medium heat, melt 100 g of margarine and whisk in 30 g of flour and 200 g of sugar until smooth.
6. Gradually stir in 350 ml of Carnation milk and the 3 beaten egg yolks, stirring constantly until thickened.
7. Remove from heat and stir in 5 ml of vanilla flavoring.
8. Pour the custard mixture over the cherries in the dish.
9. Crush the remaining 100 g of graham crackers and sprinkle over the top.
10. Refrigerate until firm before serving.

title: Duck And Sauerkraut
description: Braised duck breasts served over tangy sauerkraut, baked to tender perfection. This dish offers a rich, savory flavor contrast that is simple to prepare.
instructions:
1. Parboil 2 duck breasts in boiling water for 10 minutes to render some fat.
2. Place 500 g sauerkraut into a oven-safe casserole dish.
3. Arrange the parboiled 2 duck breasts over the sauerkraut.
4. Cover the dish tightly with a lid or aluminum foil.
5. Bake at 325°F (165°C) for 1 hour or until the duck is tender.

title: Alavna'S E Z Burritos
description: Easy beef burritos filled with a flavorful mixture of roast beef, gravy, cheese, and fresh vegetables. A convenient and satisfying meal for any day of the week.
instructions:
1. Dice 500 g beef, chop 1 green bell pepper, and dice 2 of the 3 tomatoes.
2. In a large pot over low heat, combine the 500 g beef, chopped green bell pepper, 2 diced tomatoes, 1 tsp ground cumin, 1 tsp ground garlic, 1 tsp salt, and 1 tsp pepper.
3. Cover and cook over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the bell pepper is soft.
4. Dice the remaining 1 tomato and grate 200 g Longhorn cheese.
5. Warm the 8 flour tortillas in a pan or microwave.
6. For each burrito, place a portion of the meat mixture onto a tortilla, then top with approximately 25 g grated Longhorn cheese, some of the remaining diced tomato, and 20 g shredded lettuce.
7. Roll the tortillas tightly and serve.

title: Sausage-Cheese Hors D'Oeuvres
description: Savory sausage and Velveeta cheese mixture, seasoned with Worcestershire and Tabasco, spread on party rye bread. These bite-sized hors d'oeuvres are quick to make and perfect for entertaining.
instructions:
1. Remove the skin from 450 g fresh sausage and fry the meat in a skillet until fully cooked, breaking it into small crumbles.
2. Drain off the excess fat from the skillet.
3. Stir in 1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce and 1/2 tsp Tabasco sauce into the cooked meat.
4. Cut 225 g Velveeta cheese into small cubes, add them to the sausage mixture, and stir over low heat until the cheese is completely melted.
5. Spread the cheese and sausage mixture onto slices of 1 loaf party rye bread and arrange them on a cookie sheet.
6. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 10 to 15 minutes until the bread is crisp and the topping is lightly browned.

title: Party Feta Appetizer
description: A simple and delicious feta cheese appetizer spread on toasted party rye bread. This quick broiled snack is ideal for last-minute gatherings.
instructions:
1. Finely crumble 200 g Feta cheese and chop 2 stalks green onion, 1/2 green pepper, and 1/2 red pepper.
2. In a medium bowl, combine the chopped vegetables and crumbled cheese with 120 ml mayonnaise.
3. Arrange 12 slices of party rye bread on a baking sheet and toast one side under a broiler until golden.
4. Flip the bread and spread the mayonnaise mixture evenly onto the untoasted side of each slice.
5. Place under the broiler for 1 1/2 to 2 minutes or until the topping is lightly browned and bubbly.
6. Serve immediately.

title: Scrumptious Ice Cream Torte
description: An impressive ice cream torte layered with ladyfingers, chocolate and vanilla ice cream, and Heath bar crunch. A perfect chilled dessert for any occasion, requiring some freezing time.
instructions:
1. Split 24 ladyfingers in half lengthwise and use them to line the bottom and sides of a 10 or 12-inch spring-form pan.
2. Soften 500 ml chocolate ice cream and spread it in an even layer over the ladyfingers in the pan.
3. Soften 500 ml vanilla ice cream and spread it carefully over the chocolate layer.
4. Soften 500 ml peppermint ice cream and spread it as the final ice cream layer.
5. Place the pan in the freezer for at least 8 hours or until firm.
6. Whip 250 ml heavy cream until stiff peaks form.
7. Unmold the torte and top with the whipped cream before serving.

title: Mushroom Salad
description: A fresh mushroom salad tossed with Cheddar cheese, green onions, and a simple oil and vinegar dressing. This quick and refreshing side dish is perfect for any meal.
instructions:
1. Thinly slice the 500 g mushrooms, grate the 200 g Cheddar cheese, and chop the 50 g green onions.
2. In a large bowl, toss the mushrooms, cheese, and green onions together until combined.
3. In a separate small container, whisk together the 60 ml oil, 30 ml wine vinegar, and 10 g seasoning until emulsified.
4. Pour the dressing over the mushroom mixture and toss well to coat all ingredients evenly before serving.

title: Mushroom Salad(France)
description: A classic French-inspired mushroom salad, featuring sliced mushrooms dressed with lemon, garlic, and a light mayonnaise-based dressing. This elegant side is quick and refreshing.
instructions:
1. Add 30 ml vinegar to a bowl of cold water and wash 500 g fresh mushrooms; dry them thoroughly with a cloth and slice thinly.
2. Squeeze the juice from 1 lemon over the sliced mushrooms and toss to prevent them from turning black.
3. In a separate bowl, combine 200 g mayonnaise, 1 minced clove garlic, 5 g salt, and 2 g freshly ground pepper.
4. Toss the mushrooms with the mayonnaise mixture until evenly coated.
5. Place the salad in the refrigerator for 1 to 2 hours, stirring lightly several times to incorporate the juices released by the mushrooms.
6. Arrange the leaves from 2 heads of bibb lettuce and 10 parsley sprigs on a platter.
7. Slice 2 tomatoes and place them on the greens, then top with the chilled mushroom mixture to serve.

title: Susan'S Favorite Enchiladas
description: Flavorful chicken enchiladas, simply garnished with extra cheese, sour cream, and fresh cilantro. A comforting and customizable dish for any weeknight.
instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 190°C and shred the 400 g chicken and 200 g cheese.
2. In a bowl, mix the shredded 400 g chicken with 100 g of the sour cream and 100 ml of the recipe Salsa.
3. Lay out the 8 flour tortillas and distribute the chicken mixture evenly down the center of each.
4. Roll the tortillas tightly and place them seam-side down in a baking dish.
5. Pour the remaining 400 ml recipe Salsa over the tortillas and sprinkle with the remaining 200 g cheese.
6. Bake for 20 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
7. Serve warm topped with the remaining 100 g sour cream.

title: Sauteed Butternut Squash
description: Tender sautéed butternut squash seasoned with garlic, fresh sage, and parsley. This simple side dish is perfect for a healthy and flavorful addition to any meal.
ingredients: 800 g butternut squash, peeled and cubed
instructions:
1. In a heavy nonstick skillet, heat 2 tablespoons vegetable oil over medium heat until sizzling.
2. Add 800 g cubed butternut squash and 2 cloves minced garlic to the skillet, stirring to coat thoroughly with the oil.
3. Reduce heat to low and saute for approximately 30 minutes, stirring frequently, until the squash is golden brown and tender.
4. If the squash begins to stick to the pan during cooking, add 1 or 2 tablespoons of water as needed.
5. Season with salt to taste, then sprinkle 1 tablespoon fresh sage and 1 tablespoon fresh Italian parsley over the squash.
6. Mix well to combine all flavors and serve immediately.

title: Zesty Sauerkraut
description: Zesty sauerkraut enhanced with sautéed onions, crumbled bacon, beer, molasses, and mustard. This flavorful side dish offers a unique twist on traditional sauerkraut.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 175°C (350°F).
2. Finely chop 2 large onions and 1 green pepper.
3. Melt 30 g butter in a skillet and saute the chopped onions and green pepper until softened.
4. Fry 150 g bacon until crisp, then crumble into small pieces.
5. Rinse 900 g sauerkraut and place it in a baking dish.
6. Add 250 ml beer, 60 ml molasses, and 30 g mustard to the skillet with the onions, peppers, and crumbled bacon.
7. Stir the mixture over medium heat for 3 minutes.
8. Pour the mixture over the sauerkraut in the baking dish and stir to combine.
9. Bake for 30 minutes.

title: Cherry Torte
description: A delightful cherry torte featuring a tender crust, sweet cherry pie filling, and a meringue-like topping with ground nuts. Perfect for a comforting dessert.
instructions:
1. In a large bowl, cream together 200 g butter, 150 g sugar, and 1 tsp vanilla.
2. Beat in 4 egg yolks until the mixture is smooth.
3. Stir in 0.5 tsp soda, 1 tsp baking powder, and 300 g flour to form a dough.
4. Spread the dough out on a cookie sheet and press it to make edges up the sides.
5. Cover the dough evenly with cherry pie filling.
6. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites until stiff and gradually add 6 tablespoons of sugar.
7. Spread the egg white mixture over the filling and sprinkle with ground nuts.
8. Bake at 175°C (350°F) for 25 to 30 minutes.

title: Polish Mistakes
description: Quick and savory mini open-faced sandwiches with a hot sausage and hamburger mixture, topped with melted Cheez Whiz. Broiled to perfection on party rye bread, these are great for appetizers.
instructions:
1. In a large skillet over medium-high heat, combine 500 g roll hot sausage and 500 g hamburger.
2. Fry the meats together until fully browned and cooked through, breaking them into small crumbles.
3. Drain off the excess grease thoroughly from the meat mixture.
4. Transfer the warm meat mixture to a bowl and incorporate any additional desired cheese spread or condiments while warm.
5. Spread the mixture onto party rye bread slices and broil until the bread is lightly brown and crisp.

title: Polish Pickle Soup
description: A hearty Polish pickle soup (Zupa Ogórkowa) made with pork hocks, vegetables like potatoes and cabbage, and a distinctive tang from pickle juice and dill. A comforting and traditional meal.
instructions:
1. Place 2.5 l water in a kettle and add 1 kg pork hocks and 1 large onion.
2. Cook over medium-low heat for 1 hour.
3. Remove the pork hocks and allow them to cool.
4. Once cooled, dice the meat from the hocks and set aside.
5. Add 500 g diced potato, 300 g shredded cabbage, and 2 sliced medium carrots to the kettle.
6. Cook until the vegetables are tender.
7. Stir in 250 ml pickle juice, 4 diced kosher dill pickles, 15 g dill weed, 5 g pepper, and the diced meat.
8. Cook for another 10 minutes until heated through.

title: Crisp Squash-And-Pepper Toss
description: A flavorful and colorful toss of yellow squash, red pepper, and kielbasa, seasoned with herbs and red wine vinegar. Quickly cooked, this dish makes a satisfying main or side.
instructions:
1. In a 2.5-liter casserole dish, combine 1 tsp basil, 1/2 tsp oregano, 1/2 tsp thyme, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp pepper, 30 ml red wine vinegar, and 15 ml water.
2. Slice 400 g yellow squash and add it to the mixture; cover with plastic wrap and microwave on High for 3 to 4 minutes, then let stand for 3 minutes.
3. Add 1 sliced medium onion, 1 minced clove garlic, 1 diced sweet red pepper, and 15 ml olive oil to the dish and toss gently.
4. Cover and microwave on High for 2 to 4 minutes until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
5. Slice 350 g kielbasa into rounds, add to the vegetables, and toss gently.
6. Cover and microwave on High for 1 to 2 minutes until the sausage is heated through.

title: Reuben Salad
description: A deconstructed Reuben sandwich presented as a refreshing salad with layers of sour kraut, dill pickle, roast beef, and Swiss cheese. All tossed with a savory dressing.
instructions:
1. Drain 500 g sour kraut and finely chop 3 large dill pickle and 20 g parsley. Mix these three ingredients in a bowl.
2. Wash and tear 1 head lettuce. Cut 400 g beef and 200 g swiss cheese into bite-sized strips or cubes.
3. Layer half of the lettuce at the bottom of a serving dish, followed by half of the kraut and pickle mixture, half of the beef, and half of the swiss cheese.
4. Repeat the layers with the remaining ingredients.
5. Toast 4 slices rye bread, cut them into small cubes or triangles, and sprinkle them over the top before serving.

title: Chicken-Sausage Cassoulet
description: A slow-cooked chicken and Polish sausage cassoulet, rich with vegetables, beans, and savory seasonings. This hearty one-pot meal is perfect for a comforting dinner.
instructions:
1. Chop 2 medium carrots and 1 large onion, then combine them in a saucepan with 250 ml water.
2. Simmer the mixture, covered, for 5 minutes.
3. Transfer the vegetable mixture and liquid into a crock-pot.
4. Stir in 2 tablespoons tomato paste, 120 ml red wine, 1 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/4 teaspoon ground cloves, and 2 bay leaves.
5. Add 400 g navy beans to the crock-pot and mix well.
6. Place 450 g chicken breasts and 350 g fully cooked Polish sausage on top of the beans.
7. Cover and cook on low for 9 to 10 hours or on high for 5 1/2 to 6 hours until the chicken is tender.

title: Walnut And Zucchini Stuffed Chicken
description: Succulent chicken breasts stuffed with a flavorful mixture of walnuts, zucchini, spinach, and Parmesan cheese, baked until golden and delicious. A savory and satisfying main course.
instructions:
1. Finely chop the 200 g zucchini, 1 small onion, and 50 g walnuts. Thaw and squeeze dry the 150 g frozen spinach.
2. In a medium bowl, combine 50 g Parmesan cheese, 60 g bread crumbs, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp black pepper, and the chopped walnuts; set aside.
3. Melt 30 g butter in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the zucchini, onion, and spinach. Sauté until the mixture is dry.
4. Remove the skillet from heat and stir in the cheese and bread crumb mixture until the consistency is thick.
5. Return to low heat, pour in 60 ml cream, and stir for 1 minute until creamy. Stir in 1 tbsp lemon juice and adjust seasoning if needed.
6. Carefully slide fingers under the skin of the 4 pieces chicken to create a pocket. Evenly distribute the stuffing under the skin of each piece.
7. Place the chicken skin-side up in an oiled baking dish. Bake at 175°C (350°F) for 15 minutes.
8. Baste the skin with the accumulated pan juices and bake for an additional 10 minutes until the skin is crisp and chicken is cooked through.

title: Beef Stroganoff Casserole With Biscuit Topping
description: A comforting casserole combining seasoned ground beef, onions, and garlic in a creamy mushroom and sour cream sauce, topped with a fluffy biscuit crust. A hearty family meal.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 175°C (350°F).
2. In a large skillet over medium heat, brown 500 g ground beef with 1 chopped onion, 2 cloves minced garlic, 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp celery salt, 1/2 tsp extra salt, and 1/4 tsp pepper until meat is cooked through.
3. Stir in 300 ml condensed mushroom soup and simmer for 10 minutes.
4. Remove from heat and fold in 250 ml sour cream, 150 g peas, and 150 g corn.
5. Transfer the mixture into a 2-quart baking dish and place in the oven while you prepare your biscuit dough.
6. Remove the dish briefly to arrange biscuits on top of the beef mixture.
7. Return to the oven and bake for 15-20 minutes or until the biscuits are golden brown and cooked through.

title: Braided Bread (Challah)(Makes Two Loaves)
description: Rich and slightly sweet braided challah bread, perfect for special occasions or a delicious everyday treat. This recipe yields two beautiful loaves with a golden, poppy-seed sprinkled crust.
instructions:
1. In a large bowl, dissolve 2.25 tsp active dry yeast and 60 g sugar in 350 ml very warm water.
2. Stir in 150 g of the unbleached white flour, 10 g salt, 2 eggs, and 80 ml oil.
3. Gradually add the remaining unbleached white flour, stirring until the mixture is combined.
4. Transfer the dough to a floured surface and knead for 8 to 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
5. Place the dough in an oil-coated bowl, turning once to coat, cover with a cloth, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
6. Punch the dough down, flip it over, cover again, and let it rise for an additional 45 minutes.
