7. Punch the dough down again, place on a floured surface, and divide into two equal portions.
8. Braid the loaves, brush with the 1 remaining beaten egg, sprinkle with 1 tbsp poppy seed, and bake at 190°C for 30-35 minutes.

title: Flavored Mayo
description: A quick and versatile flavored mayonnaise, perfect for sandwiches, dips, or as a creamy addition to casseroles. Easily blended in minutes for an instant flavor boost.
instructions:
1. Mince the 2 cloves garlic finely.
2. In a small bowl or blender, combine the 250 ml mayo, 15 ml lemon juice, minced garlic, 1/2 tsp ground pepper, and 1/2 tsp dry mustard.
3. Blend or whisk the mixture thoroughly until completely smooth.
4. Transfer the flavored mayo to a tight jar and store in the refrigerator until ready to serve.

title: Easy Meatloaf
description: An easy-to-prepare meatloaf using lean hamburger, seasoned and baked until tender. A classic comfort food that's simple to assemble for a weeknight dinner.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C).
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine 500 g extra lean hamburger, 30 g meatloaf seasoning, 1 egg, 120 ml applesauce, and 60 g breadcrumbs.
3. Mix all ingredients thoroughly until well incorporated.
4. Place the mixture into a loaf pan or shape it into a loaf in a large baking dish, ensuring there is space on all sides for even cooking.
5. Bake in the preheated oven for 1 hour until cooked through.

title: Quick Russian Tea
description: A warm and fragrant beverage made from a blend of lemonade mix, cinnamon, cloves, and sugar. This quick Russian tea mixture is perfect for a comforting drink on a cold day.
instructions:
1. Combine 200 g lemonade mix, 100 g sugar, 2 tsp cinnamon, and 1 tsp ground cloves in a mixing bowl.
2. Mix all ingredients together thoroughly until the spices are evenly distributed throughout the sugar and lemonade base.
3. Transfer the mixture into a tightly closed container for storage.
4. When serving, stir 2 to 3 heaping teaspoons of the mixture into a cup of hot water until fully dissolved.

title: Reuben Appetizers
description: Savory Reuben-inspired appetizers with party rye bread, corned beef, sauerkraut, Swiss cheese, and dressing, broiled until warm and cheesy. Perfect for a quick snack or party starter.
instructions:
1. Preheat the broiler and arrange 30 slices party rye bread in a single layer on a large cookie sheet.
2. Divide 200 g thin sliced corned beef evenly among the bread slices.
3. Drain 250 g sauerkraut thoroughly and place a small spoonful over the beef on each slice.
4. Top each appetizer with a piece of 200 g Swiss cheese.
5. Place the tray about 6 inches away from the heat source and broil for 2 to 3 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbly.

title: Quick Borsch
description: A quick and refreshing chilled borscht, blending beets with consommé, celery soup, and horseradish. This vibrant soup is perfect for a light and flavorful meal, garnished with sour cream.
instructions:
1. Place 400 g beets into a blender with half of their liquid.
2. Add 500 ml consomme, 300 ml cream of celery soup, 1 tbsp horseradish, and 1/2 tsp garlic powder to the blender.
3. Blend all ingredients until the mixture is completely smooth.
4. Transfer the mixture to a container and chill in the refrigerator for at least 3 hours.
5. Serve chilled, optionally garnished with sour cream or herbs.

title: Jean'S Mess
description: A hearty one-pot meal featuring browned ground beef, onion, and garlic simmered in a creamy blend of condensed soups. This comforting dish is simple to prepare for a satisfying dinner.
instructions:
1. In a large pot, brown 500 g lean ground beef with 1 chopped onion and 2 minced cloves garlic until the meat is cooked through.
2. Drain any excess fat from the pot.
3. Stir in 300 ml cream of celery soup, 300 ml cream of mushroom soup, 300 ml cream of chicken soup, 300 ml vegetable with, 300 ml chicken gumbo soup, and 200 g cooked diced chicken.
4. Cook the mixture over low heat for 45 minutes or until the sauce is hot and bubbly.
5. Stir occasionally to prevent the mixture from sticking and serve over noodles or rice.

title: Jimmy Dean Breakfast Burrito
description: Flavorful breakfast burritos filled with seasoned sausage, onions, potatoes, and scrambled eggs, all wrapped in a warm flour tortilla. A delicious and satisfying way to start your day.
instructions:
1. Crumble 450 g sausage and 1 finely diced medium onion into a large skillet over medium-high heat. Brown until meat is cooked through and onions are translucent.
2. Drain any excess fat from the skillet.
3. Add 2 peeled and diced medium potatoes to the skillet and cook until tender. Whisk 6 large eggs and pour them over the sausage mixture, scrambling everything together until the eggs are fully set.
4. Steam 6 flour tortillas until soft and flexible.
5. Divide the cooked mixture evenly among the tortillas, rolling them tightly into burritos to serve.

title: Reuben Rolls
description: Savory Reuben-inspired rolls with a delicious filling of corned beef, Swiss cheese, and sauerkraut, enveloped in pizza dough and baked until golden. A fun and easy dish for snacking or a light meal.
instructions:
1. Finely chop or shred 200 g corn beef and 150 g Swiss cheese.
2. In a medium bowl, combine the chopped corn beef, 150 g Swiss cheese, and 200 g sauerkraut (drained).
3. Stir 30 ml Miracle and 15 ml mustard into the mixture until well blended.
4. Roll out 400 g dough into a large rectangle on a lightly floured surface.
5. Spread the meat and cheese mixture evenly over the surface of the dough.
6. Roll the dough up tightly like a jelly roll and place on a cookie sheet.
7. Bake at 218°C (425°F) for 10 minutes or at 175°C (350°F) for 25 minutes until golden brown.

title: Cheese Potato Dish
description: A comforting potato casserole combining frozen hash browns, creamy chicken soup, Cheddar cheese, and onion, baked until bubbly and topped with a buttery, crispy crust. A perfect side dish.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 175°C (350°F).
2. Finely dice 1 medium onion and shred 250 g Cheddar cheese.
3. In a large mixing bowl, combine 900 g frozen hash browns, 300 ml cream of chicken soup, 250 g shredded Cheddar cheese, 1 diced onion, 250 ml sour cream, and salt to taste.
4. Spread the mixture evenly into a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan.
5. Melt 60 g margarine and drizzle it evenly over the top of the hash brown mixture.
6. Bake for 1 hour until the top is golden and bubbly.

title: German Potato Pancakes (Kartoffelpuffer)
description: Traditional German Kartoffelpuffer, crisp potato pancakes seasoned with onion, egg, and nutmeg, perfect for a savory snack or side dish. These golden delights are pan-fried until perfection.
instructions:
1. Peel and finely grate 1 kg potatoes and 1 large onion, then squeeze out excess moisture using a clean kitchen towel.
2. Finely chop 100 g bacon and 15 g fresh parsley.
3. In a large bowl, whisk 2 large eggs, then stir in 50 g flour, 10 g salt, 15 g chopped parsley, and 1 pinch nutmeg until smooth.
4. Add the grated potatoes, grated onion, and 100 g chopped bacon to the egg mixture and stir thoroughly to combine.
5. Heat 100 ml vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
6. Drop portions of the mixture into the hot oil, flattening them into pancakes, and fry for 3-5 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy.

title: Clam Mushroom Salad
description: A refreshing clam and mushroom salad with fresh lettuce, red onion, and tomato, dressed in a tangy clam juice vinaigrette. A light and flavorful salad for any meal.
instructions:
1. Core, rinse, and drain 1 large head country stand lettuce.
2. Place lettuce in a plastic bag and chill until crisp.
3. Clean mushrooms with damp paper towels, trim stems, and slice lengthwise.
4. In a large jar, combine 120 ml clam juice, 180 ml corn oil, 60 ml vinegar, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1 tsp dried tarragon.
5. Cover the jar and shake well to emulsify the dressing.
6. Slice 1 small red onion thinly and add it along with the prepared mushrooms to the dressing.
7. Shake to coat the vegetables and chill in the refrigerator.
8. Tear the chilled lettuce into bite-sized pieces to yield 5 cups.
9. Cut 2 large tomatoes into wedges.
10. In a large salad bowl, combine the lettuce, tomato wedges, and the mushroom mixture.
11. Toss lightly to combine and serve immediately.

title: Polish Sausage And Lentils
description: Hearty and flavorful combination of Polish sausage and tender lentils, simmered with onions, garlic, tomatoes, and bay leaf. A comforting and satisfying meal perfect for a chilly evening.
instructions:
1. Place 200 g rinsed lentils and 1 unit bay leaf in a saucepan, cover with water, add a pinch of salt, and simmer until tender, about 20 minutes.
2. While lentils cook, slice 400 g polish sausage and chop 2 medium onions.
3. Heat 30 ml vegetable oil in a skillet and sauté the chopped onions and sliced sausage until browned.
4. Stir in 400 g diced tomatoes, 3 whole cloves, 5 g sugar, and the to taste pepper.
5. Drain the cooked lentils and add them to the skillet with the sausage mixture.
6. Simmer everything together for 10 minutes to meld flavors, adjust the to taste salt if needed, and serve.

title: Sweet And Sour Kielbasa Casserole
description: A delightful sweet and sour kielbasa casserole featuring sliced kielbasa, butter beans, and an tangy sauce with tomato soup, brown sugar, and ketchup. Baked until bubbly and flavorful.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 175°C (350°F).
2. Finely dice 1 medium onion and 1 large green pepper.
3. In a large bowl, combine 2 cans (400g each) butter beans, 300 ml tomato soup, the diced onion, the diced green pepper, 50 g brown sugar, and 60 ml ketchup. Stir until well mixed.
4. Pour the bean mixture into a 2-liter casserole dish.
5. Slice 500 g kielbasa into 1/4-inch rounds and layer them evenly over the top of the mixture.
6. Bake uncovered for 60 minutes until the sauce is bubbly and the kielbasa is browned.

title: Kiddie Chow
description: A delicious kiddie chow recipe.
instructions:
1. In a large microwave-safe bowl or heavy saucepan, melt 350 g chocolate chips and 60 g butter until smooth.
2. Stir 125 g peanut butter into the melted chocolate mixture until fully incorporated.
3. Gently fold in 200 g Bran until every piece is evenly coated with the chocolate mixture.
4. Transfer the mixture to a parchment-lined pan and pat into an even layer.
5. Once the mixture has cooled and hardened, sprinkle 100 g powdered sugar over the top.

title: Coconut Bars
description: A delicious coconut bars recipe.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Cream 115 g butter and 100 g brown sugar, then stir in 125 g flour.
2. Press the mixture into a baking pan and bake for 20 minutes.
3. In a separate bowl, beat 2 eggs with 200 g brown sugar, 30 g flour, 1/2 tsp salt, 1 tsp vanilla, and 1/2 tsp baking powder until well combined.
4. Fold 100 g chopped nuts into the egg mixture.
5. Spread the mixture evenly over the baked base.
6. Sprinkle 150 g coconut over the top.
7. Bake at 325°F (165°C) for 25 minutes.
8. Allow to cool completely before cutting into bars.

title: Jiffy Stroganoff
description: A delicious jiffy stroganoff recipe.
instructions:
1. In a large skillet over medium-high heat, brown 500 g hamburger until cooked through.
2. Add 1 chopped large onion and 250 g sliced mushrooms to the skillet, sautéing until the vegetables are softened.
3. Stir in 15 ml Worcestershire sauce, 30 ml ketchup, and 1/2 tsp salt to season the mixture.
4. Lower heat and stir in 300 ml condensed cream and 240 ml sour cream, mixing until a smooth sauce forms.
5. Simmer the mixture gently for 5 minutes until heated through; do not boil.
6. While the sauce simmers, boil 400 g egg noodles in salted water according to package directions, then drain.
7. Serve the meat and sauce mixture hot over the cooked egg noodles.

title: Holiday Fondue Pot Or Microwave
description: A delicious holiday fondue pot or microwave recipe.
instructions:
1. Grate 250 g Gruyere cheese and 250 g Swiss cheese; toss in a bowl with 2 tbsp flour and 1/2 tsp dry mustard.
2. Rub the inside of a heavy pot or fondue pot with 1 clove garlic, then discard the clove.
3. Pour 250 ml white wine and 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce into the pot and bring to a slow simmer over medium heat.
4. Gradually add the cheese mixture one handful at a time, stirring constantly in a figure-eight motion until the cheese is melted and smooth.
5. If using a microwave, heat the wine and Worcestershire sauce in a casserole dish for 1.5 minutes on high, stir in the cheese mixture, then microwave at 50% power for 7 to 9 minutes, stirring every 2 minutes.
6. Serve immediately with bread or vegetables.

title: Tortellini In White Sauce
description: A delicious tortellini in white sauce recipe.
instructions:
1. In a large skillet over medium heat, melt 50 g butter and sauté 200 g sliced sausage until browned.
2. Pour in 500 ml hot milk and bring the mixture to a slow boil.
3. Whisk in 30 g flour, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/4 tsp pepper.
4. Reduce heat to low and cook, stirring constantly, until the liquid begins to thicken.
5. Stir in 2 containers heavy cream and continue to simmer on low for 8 minutes to thicken the sauce.
6. While sauce simmers, boil 500 g tortellini in a separate pot according to package directions, then drain and toss with the white sauce.

title: Tortoni Squares
description: A delicious tortoni squares recipe.
instructions:
1. Melt 60 g oleo and combine with 300 g vanilla wafer crumbs, 100 g chopped almonds, and 1 tsp almond flavoring; mix well.
2. Sprinkle 1/3 of the crumb mixture over the bottom of a foil-lined 8 1/2 x 11-inch pan.
3. Carefully spoon 500 ml of vanilla ice cream over the crumbs and spread evenly.
4. Drizzle 125 ml of apricot preserves over the ice cream layer.
5. Sprinkle another 1/3 of the crumbs over the preserves.
6. Layer the remaining 500 ml of vanilla ice cream, followed by the remaining 125 ml of apricot preserves.
7. Top with the final 1/3 of crumbs and store in the freezer until firm.
8. Cut into squares to serve.

title: Stroganoff Style Pork Chops
description: A delicious stroganoff style pork chops recipe.
instructions:
1. Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat and brown 4 pork chops for 5 minutes per side.
2. Remove pork chops from the skillet and set aside to keep warm.
3. In the remaining drippings, sauté 1 chopped onion and 250 g sliced mushrooms until tender.
4. Stir in 125 ml water, 1 tbsp mustard, and 1/2 tsp salt; bring the mixture to a boil.
5. Return the pork chops to the pan, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until chops are tender.
6. Remove the pork chops again and keep warm.
7. In a small bowl, whisk together 2 tbsp flour and 200 ml sour cream until smooth, then stir the mixture into the skillet juices.
8. Bring sauce to a boil and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes until thickened.
9. Serve the pork chops and mushroom sauce over 500 g hot cooked egg noodles.

title: Pork Chop Plus
description: A delicious pork chop plus recipe.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Slice 4 large potatoes and 2 medium onions into thin rounds.
3. Place 6 center cut pork chops in the bottom of a large casserole pan.
4. Layer the sliced potatoes and onions evenly over the pork chops.
5. Sprinkle 1 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/2 tsp pepper over the vegetables.
6. Pour 500 ml tomato juice over all ingredients until covered.
7. Cover the pan and bake for 1.5 hours or until potatoes are tender.

title: Chocolate Sin Cake
description: A delicious chocolate sin cake recipe.
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 175°C (350°F) and grease/flour a Bundt or tube pan.
2. In a small bowl, toss 340 g chocolate chips and 120 g chopped pecans with 1 tablespoon of the 432 g chocolate cake mix until coated.
3. In a large mixing bowl, combine the remaining chocolate cake mix, 4 eggs, 120 ml oil, 5 ml vanilla, 110 g instant chocolate pudding, and 240 ml sour cream.
4. Beat the mixture for 3 minutes until smooth and well combined.
5. Gently fold the coated chocolate chips and pecans into the batter.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 50 minutes.
7. Cool the cake on a wire rack for 10 to 15 minutes before transferring to a cake plate.
8. Heat 240 ml Chocolate Shoppe topping until it is warm and fluid, then pour it over the cake before serving.

title: Blueberry Torte
description: A delicious blueberry torte recipe.
instructions:
1. Mix 200 g graham cracker crumbs, 100 g sugar, and 115 g melted oleo in a bowl.
2. Press the crust mixture into the bottom of a 9 x 13-inch pan.
3. Beat 450 g cream cheese with 150 g sugar and 2 eggs until smooth.
4. Pour the cream cheese mixture over the crust and bake if required by your preference, or chill until firm.
5. Layer 600 g blueberry pie topping and 200 g fresh blueberries over the cream cheese layer.
6. Prepare 1 packet Dream Whip according to package directions and spread over the top before serving.

title: Kona Banana Bread
description: A delicious kona banana bread recipe.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease a 9x9x2-inch baking pan.
2. In a large bowl, cream together 120 g shortening and 200 g sugar until light and fluffy.
3. Beat in 2 eggs one at a time until well combined.
4. Stir in 400 g mashed ripe bananas.
5. Gradually add 250 g flour and mix until just combined.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 30 to 35 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean.

title: Stuffed Cabbage In Tomato Sauce
description: A delicious stuffed cabbage in tomato sauce recipe.
instructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix 500 g lean ground beef, 200 g rice, 1 finely chopped onion, 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp pepper, and 1 egg until well blended.
2. Boil 1 cabbage briefly to soften leaves, then remove 12 large leaves and trim the thick stem from each.
3. Divide the meat mixture into 12 portions and wrap each in a cabbage leaf, fastening with toothpicks if needed.
4. Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large skillet or pot and brown the cabbage rolls on all sides.
5. Pour in 500 ml tomato sauce and 60 ml water over the rolls.
6. Cover the pot and simmer on low heat for 40 minutes until the rice and meat are fully cooked.

title: Beef And Sausage Hors D'Oeuvre
description: A delicious beef and sausage hors d'oeuvre recipe.
instructions:
1. In a large skillet over medium heat, brown 500 g ground beef and 500 g ground pork until fully cooked.
2. Drain the excess fat from the skillet.
3. Add 450 g cubed Velveeta cheese, 1 tsp oregano, 1 tsp garlic salt, and 1 tsp chili powder to the meat mixture.
4. Stir constantly until the cheese is completely melted and the ingredients are well combined.
5. Preheat the oven to 175°C and line a cookie sheet with aluminum foil.
6. Place the slices of cocktail rye bread on the cookie sheet.
7. Spread a generous tablespoon of the meat and cheese mixture onto each slice of bread.
8. Bake for 10-12 minutes or until the bread is toasted and the topping is bubbly.

title: Baline (Potato Pancake)
description: A delicious baline (potato pancake) recipe.
instructions:
1. Peel and grate 1 kg potatoes and 1 medium onion fine, or process them in a blender.
2. In a large bowl, combine the potato and onion mixture with 1 large egg, 5 g baking powder, 50 g flour, and salt to taste.
3. Mix thoroughly until the batter is consistent.
4. Heat a layer of fat or oil in a pan and drop spoonfuls of the mixture to shape into patties.
5. Fry the patties over medium heat until they are golden brown and crispy on both sides.

title: Party Spinach Dip(Low-Fat)
description: A delicious party spinach dip(low-fat) recipe.
instructions:
1. Finely chop 1 medium onion and drain 225 g water chestnuts before chopping them into small pieces.
2. Place 500 g cottage cheese into a blender and process until completely smooth.
3. Transfer the smooth cottage cheese to a large mixing bowl and add 250 ml sour cream.
4. Stir in the chopped onion, 30 g vegetable soup mix, 15 ml lemon juice, and the chopped water chestnuts.
5. Blend all ingredients together well until thoroughly combined.
6. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 3 hours before serving to allow flavors to meld.

title: Polish Meatballs
description: A delicious polish meatballs recipe.
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 175°C (350°F).
2. In a large bowl, mix 500 g ground beef, 100 g bread crumbs, 40 g onion soup, and 2 eggs until well combined.
3. Form the beef mixture into very small meatballs and place them in an oblong baking pan.
4. In a separate bowl, whisk together 250 ml chili sauce, 200 g cranberries, 100 g brown sugar, 400 g sauerkraut, and 120 ml water.
5. Pour the sauce mixture evenly over the meatballs in the pan.
6. Cover the pan with foil and bake for 1 hour.
7. Remove the foil and continue baking for another 30 to 60 minutes until the meatballs are cooked through and the sauce is bubbly.

title: Mexican Stack Ups
description: A delicious mexican stack ups recipe.
instructions:
1. Brown 500 g lean hamburger in a skillet over medium-high heat and drain the excess fat.
2. Stir 1 packet taco seasoning into the browned hamburger according to the package directions.
3. Heat 400 g beans in a medium pan over low heat until warm.
4. Microwave 12 flour tortillas for 3 to 4 minutes in their package, turning once.
5. Layer a tortilla on a plate and spread with approximately 30 g of beans and 40 g of meat.
6. Top the layer with shredded Cheddar, diced tomatoes, and diced onions.
7. Place a second tortilla on top and repeat the layers of beans, meat, cheese, tomatoes, and onions.

title: Mexican Blintzes
description: A delicious mexican blintzes recipe.
instructions:
1. Cut 500 g cheese into slices approximately 3 x 1 1/4 inches.
2. Cut 150 g green chilies into thin strips.
3. Heat 24 flour tortillas until warm and pliable.
4. Place one slice of cheese and one chili strip in the center of each tortilla.
5. Fold over the ends and roll up tightly, securing each with a toothpick.
6. Heat 500 ml salad oil to 400°F (200°C) and deep fry the blintzes in small batches until golden brown.
7. Drain on paper towels and remove the toothpicks.
8. Cut the blintzes in half crosswise and stand them on their ends to prevent the cheese from running out.
9. Serve immediately with 250 ml salsa for dipping.

title: Easy French Onion Rice
description: A delicious easy french onion rice recipe.
instructions:
1. In a large skillet or saucepan, melt 50 g butter over medium heat.
2. Mince 2 clove garlic and sauté in the butter until fragrant.
3. Add 350 g Uncle Ben's rice to the pan and stir until the grains are coated and slightly toasted.
4. Pour in 300 ml Campbell French Onion soup, 200 ml water, and 100 ml White wine.
5. Add 200 g mushrooms (including juice if canned) to the mixture and stir well.
6. Bring the liquid to a boil, then stir once more.
7. Reduce heat to low, cover the pan, and simmer for 20 minutes or until the liquid is fully absorbed.

title: Sugar Cream Pie(My Mother-In-Law'S Recipe)
description: A delicious sugar cream pie(my mother-in-law's recipe) recipe.
instructions:
1. Mix 200 g sugar, 1/4 tsp salt, and 35 g flour together in a saucepan.
2. Gradually add 473 ml of half and half into the pan while stirring.
3. Add 30 g butter (the size of a walnut) to the mixture.
4. Place the pan over heat and bring the mixture to a boil.
5. Cook the mixture until it becomes thick, then cool it slightly.
6. Pour the mixture into one unbaked 9-inch pie shell.
7. Bake at 175°C (350°F) until the top is brown.

title: Skillet Pork And Sauerkraut
description: A delicious skillet pork and sauerkraut recipe.
instructions:
1. Broil the 2 lean pork chops on a preheated broiler 4 inches from heat for 2 minutes on each side.
2. Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
3. Finely dice the 1 medium onion and mince the 2 cloves garlic, then add them to the skillet and cook until softened.
4. Stir in the 500 g sauerkraut, 120 ml apple juice, 1 tsp caraway seed, 1/2 tsp thyme, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp freshly ground black pepper, and 1/4 cup of water.
5. Place the pork chops on top of the mixture, cover, and simmer on low heat for about 1 hour or until chops are tender.
6. Slice the 1 medium apple, stir the slices into the skillet, and simmer for an additional 3 minutes before serving.

title: Crisp Onion-Roasted Potatoes
description: A delicious crisp onion-roasted potatoes recipe.
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 230°C (450°F).
2. In a shallow baking pan, combine 1 chopped large onion, 60 ml olive oil, 30 g melted margarine, 1 tsp dried thyme, 1 tsp dried marjoram, and 1 tsp salt.
3. Peel and cube 1.5 kg all-purpose potatoes, then add them to the pan.
4. Toss the potatoes thoroughly until they are evenly coated with the herb and onion mixture.
5. Bake for approximately 45 to 60 minutes, turning occasionally, until the potatoes are tender and golden brown.

title: Tuna Stroganoff
description: A delicious tuna stroganoff recipe.
ingredients: 1 can (300 ml) cream of mushroom soup
instructions:
1. Melt 30 g butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Finely chop 1 onion and slice 250 g mushrooms, then sauté them in the butter until the onion is translucent and mushrooms are browned.
3. Stir in 1 can (300 ml) of cream of mushroom soup and bring to a gentle simmer.
4. Drain 2 cans of tuna fish and flake the meat into the skillet, stirring to combine thoroughly.
5. Reduce heat to low and stir in 200 g sour cream; heat through for 2-3 minutes without reaching a boil.
6. Serve immediately over noodles or rice.

title: Sauerkraut And Meat
description: A delicious sauerkraut and meat recipe.
instructions:
1. Fry 4 slices of bacon in a skillet until brown and crisp, then remove and drain on paper towels.
2. Dice 1 large onion and saute it in the remaining bacon drippings until translucent.
3. In a 9x13-inch pan, combine 900 g sauerkraut, the cooked bacon, the sautéed onion, and 30 g pimento.
4. In a small pot, heat 250 ml water and dissolve 1 chicken bouillon cube, 50 g brown sugar, and 1 tsp caraway seed.
5. Pour the liquid mixture evenly over the sauerkraut in the pan.
6. Arrange 4 pork chops on top of the sauerkraut mixture.
7. Cover the pan and bake at 350°F (175°C) for 1 1/2 hours.

title: Orange Mushroom Salad
description: A delicious orange mushroom salad recipe.
instructions:
1. Peel and slice 2 oranges into segments, and thinly slice 1 small red onion.
2. In a large bowl, combine 300 g torn iceberg lettuce, the orange segments, 200 g country stand, and the sliced onion.
3. Pour 100 ml French dressing over the mixture and toss until all components are well coated.
4. Transfer the salad to a serving dish and top with 50 g raisins before serving.

title: Potato Stuffing Casserole
description: A delicious potato stuffing casserole recipe.
instructions:
1. Chop 2 stalks celery and 1 medium onion.
2. In a medium skillet, saute the celery and onion in 2 tbsp butter until tender.
3. Add 4 cups bread cubes and stir until the bread is lightly browned.
4. In a large bowl, combine the bread mixture with 4 cups cooked, diced potatoes, 2 tbsp fresh parsley, 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp pepper, 120 ml hot milk, and 1 beaten egg.
5. Mix all ingredients thoroughly to ensure the potatoes and bread are well coated.
6. Spoon the mixture into a greased 1 1/2-quart baking dish.
7. Place the remaining 2 tbsp butter in small pieces over the top of the mixture.
8. Bake uncovered at 350°F (175°C) for 30 to 40 minutes until the top is golden brown.

title: Tosca Torte(Finland-Sweden)
description: A delicious tosca torte(finland-sweden) recipe.
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 160°C (325°F) and grease a round cake pan.
2. In a large bowl, whisk 3 eggs and 150 g sugar until the mixture becomes light and foamy.
3. Sift together 150 g flour and 1 tsp baking powder, then fold this gently into the egg mixture.
4. Melt 100 g butter over low heat and allow it to cool slightly.
5. Stir 50 ml cream and the melted 100 g butter into the batter until well combined.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for approximately 20 minutes until half-baked.

title: Chicken Milano(An Easy Make-Ahead Casserole)
description: A delicious chicken milano(an easy make-ahead casserole) recipe.
instructions:
1. Dice 500 g boneless chicken, 1 onion, 2 stalks celery, and 1 green pepper into bite-sized pieces.
2. Saute the prepared chicken, onion, celery, and green pepper in oil for 4 minutes.
3. Slice 250 g mushrooms and add them to the pan along with 15 ml Worcestershire sauce, 1/2 tsp pepper, and 1 tsp basil.
4. Simmer the mixture for 20 minutes over medium-low heat.
5. Stir in 300 ml cream of mushroom soup and 120 ml milk, blending well, and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
6. Boil 250 g fresh linguine according to package directions, drain, and toss with the chicken and sauce mixture.
7. Pour the combined mixture into a 2-quart casserole dish.
8. Slice 2 tomatoes and overlap the slices on top of the casserole.
9. Melt 30 g butter, toss with 100 g croutons, and spread them over the tomato layer.
10. Bake at 175°C (350°F) for 20 minutes.
11. Sprinkle 50 g Romano cheese over the top and bake for 10 more minutes until golden.

title: Brownie Chocolate Cake
description: A delicious brownie chocolate cake recipe.
instructions:
1. Sift 400 g sugar, 250 g flour, and 1/2 tsp salt together into a large mixing bowl.
2. In a saucepan over medium heat, combine 225 g butter, 40 g cocoa, and 240 ml water.
3. Bring the butter mixture to a boil, then remove from heat.
4. Stir the boiled mixture into the sifted dry ingredients and mix well.
5. In a separate bowl, beat 2 eggs until lightly blended.
6. Add 1 tsp vanilla, 1 tsp soda, and 120 ml buttermilk to the beaten eggs.
7. Stir the buttermilk mixture into the first batter until smooth.
8. Pour the batter into a greased sheet cake pan.
9. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 20 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.

title: *Stuffed Cabbage With Lemon Sauce
description: A delicious *stuffed cabbage with lemon sauce recipe.
instructions:
1. Place 1 large head cabbage in a pot of boiling water for 10 minutes, then carefully separate the leaves.
2. In a bowl, combine 500 g hamburger, 200 g rice, 1 finely chopped medium onion, 1 tsp salt, 0.5 tsp pepper, and 2 tbsp parsley.
3. Place about 2 tablespoons of the meat mixture into each cabbage leaf.
4. Loosely fold the sides of the leaf over the filling and roll it up tightly.
5. Layer the cabbage rolls into a large pot close together to prevent unrolling.
6. Dot the top with 50 g butter and pour in 700 ml water.
7. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 60 to 90 minutes until tender.

title: Roasted Carrot And Beet Soup
description: A delicious roasted carrot and beet soup recipe.
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 175°C (350°F).
2. Chop 4 large carrots, 2 stalks celery, and 1 medium onion and place them in a shallow oven-proof dish.
3. Sprinkle the vegetables with 25 g brown sugar, 1/2 tsp ground ginger, and 1/4 tsp ground mace.
4. Pour 500 ml of chicken broth into the dish and cover tightly with aluminum foil.
5. Bake for 2 hours until tender, then transfer the vegetables and cooking liquid into a large pot.
6. Add the remaining 1 l of chicken broth to the pot and bring to a boil.
7. Reduce heat to medium and simmer partially covered for 10 minutes, then let it cool slightly.
8. Place the mixture and 2 large cooked beets into a blender and process until smooth.
9. Return the soup to the pot and reheat if necessary.
10. Serve hot garnished with 100 ml sour cream and 15 g chopped fresh chives.

title: Enchiladas(Joe And Sonya Kuykendall, 11-3-78)
description: A delicious enchiladas(joe and sonya kuykendall, 11-3-78) recipe.
instructions:
1. Combine 250 ml tomato sauce and 1 packet enchilada mix in a pan. Place over low heat.
2. Finely chop 1 medium onion. Brown 500 g ground beef and the onion in a skillet over low heat until cooked through.
3. After the sauce has simmered for 10 minutes, add some of it to the beef mixture, using just enough to reach a slightly soupy consistency.
4. Warm 12 corn tortillas. Fill each with the beef mixture, 1 can chili, and slices from 250 g Velveeta cheese.
5. Roll the tortillas, place in a dish, top with remaining sauce and cheese, then heat until melted.

title: Chicken  Swirl Slices
description: A delicious chicken  swirl slices recipe.
instructions:
1. Heat oven to 350°.
2. Place 4 chicken breast halves between plastic wrap and pound to 1/8 inch thickness.
3. Melt 2 tbsp margarine and brush onto each chicken breast.
4. Combine 1 tsp marjoram leaves, 1 tbsp thyme, and 1 tsp rosemary; sprinkle the mixture over the chicken.
5. Layer 4 thin slices Swiss cheese over the herbed chicken and roll up tightly, securing with toothpicks.
6. Place rolls in a baking dish, pour 120 ml chicken broth over them, and bake for 20-25 minutes.
7. Serve with 100 ml Country Mustard Sauce and 12 slices cocktail rye bread.

title: Swedish Rye Bread(Bread Machine Recipe)
description: A delicious swedish rye bread(bread machine recipe) recipe.
instructions:
1. Mix 150 g rye flour, 350 g bread flour, 2 tbsp sugar, 1.5 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp cardamon seed, 1/2 tsp anise seed, and the zest of 1 orange in a bowl and set aside.
2. Pour 300 ml warm water and 80 ml molasses into the bread machine pan.
3. Add the mixed dry ingredients, 2.25 tsp rapid rise yeast, and 30 g softened butter to the pan.
4. Set the machine to the dough cycle; monitor the kneading process and add tiny amounts of water or flour if needed to reach a soft consistency.
5. When the cycle ends, turn dough onto a floured surface and let it rest for 15 minutes.
6. Shape the dough into a single round loaf.
7. Sprinkle 1 tbsp corn Meal onto a lightly greased baking sheet and place the loaf on top.
8. Cover with a towel and let rise until doubled in size while preheating the oven to 190°C (375°F).
9. Bake the loaf for 30 to 35 minutes until it sounds hollow when tapped on the bottom.
10. Transfer to a wire rack and cool for 15 minutes before slicing.

title: Polish Easter Cake
description: A delicious polish easter cake recipe.
instructions:
1. Scald 250 ml milk in a saucepan, then remove from heat.
2. Stir 150 g confectioners sugar, a pinch of salt, and 100 g butter into the hot milk until melted.
3. Let the milk mixture cool until it is lukewarm.
4. Pour 60 ml warm water into a large bowl, sprinkle 14 g yeast over it, and stir until dissolved.
5. Add the lukewarm milk mixture, 200 ml additional milk, 3 eggs, and 500 g flour to the yeast; beat vigorously for 5 minutes.
6. Cover the bowl and let the dough rise in a warm place for 1.5 hours or until doubled in size.
7. Stir down the risen batter to release air.
8. Beat in 50 g almonds, 100 g raisins, and 1 tbsp lemon peel until evenly distributed.
9. Pour the batter into a greased and floured mold, topping with 50 g candied cherries.
10. Let rise for 1 hour, then bake at 350°F (175°C) for 50 minutes.
11. Cool in the pan for 20 minutes before removing the cake.

title: Clainy'S Frogmore Stew
description: A delicious clainy's frogmore stew recipe.
instructions:
1. In a large Dutch oven, bring 4 liters of water to a boil with 60 ml Bay seasoning and 5 g salt.
2. Add 900 g red potatoes to the pot and return to a boil.
3. Slice 450 g kielbasa into rounds and cut 4 ears of corn into halves or thirds; add both to the boiling water.
4. Cook for 10 minutes or until the potatoes are fork-tender.
5. Add 900 g shrimp, 2 g cayenne pepper, 30 ml lemon juice, and the remaining 15 ml Bay seasoning.
6. Cook for 3 to 5 minutes until the shrimp turn pink and opaque.
7. Use a slotted spoon to transfer all ingredients to a large serving platter and serve immediately.

title: Microwave Chex Party Mix
description: A quick and easy savory snack mix, perfect for parties or anytime munching. This recipe features cereals, nuts, and pretzels coated in a buttery, seasoned sauce and baked until crispy.
instructions:
1. Place 60 g butter in a microwave-safe container and melt on High for 30 seconds.
2. Stir 5 g salt and 30 ml Worcestershire sauce into the melted butter and mix well.
3. Combine 250 g cereal, 150 g mixed nuts, and 100 g pretzels in a large plastic bag.
4. Pour the butter mixture over the dry ingredients in the bag and shake until everything is evenly coated.
5. Transfer the mixture into a large microwave-safe bowl.
6. Microwave on High for 3 minutes total, stopping to stir thoroughly every 60 seconds.
7. Spread the mixture onto paper towels and allow it to cool completely before serving.

title: Mushroom Sauce(Served On Christmas Eve Over Varenyky-Pyrohy)
description: A rich and savory mushroom sauce traditionally served over varenyky (Ukrainian dumplings). This dish brings earthy flavors to your holiday table, especially on Christmas Eve.
instructions:
1. Rinse 50 g mushrooms and soak in a small amount of water for 10 minutes.
2. Finely chop the soaked mushrooms and 200 g fresh mushrooms.
3. Place the chopped soaked mushrooms in a saucepan and add 500 ml cold water.
4. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to simmer and add the 200 g fresh mushrooms.
5. Cook for approximately 30 minutes until all mushrooms are soft.
6. In a separate pan, sauté 1 chopped onion in 60 g butter until translucent.
7. Stir in 2 tbsp flour to the butter and onion mixture to create a roux.
8. Gradually whisk in 250 ml mushroom stock and the liquid from the cooked mushrooms.
9. Simmer the sauce until thickened and combine with the cooked mushrooms before serving.

title: Quick Hearty Nacho Dip
description: This quick and hearty nacho dip combines seasoned ground beef, beans, and picante sauce, topped with melted Cheddar cheese. It's a perfect appetizer for gatherings, served hot with tortilla chips.
instructions:
1. Brown 500 g ground beef with 1 chopped medium onion and 1 chopped green pepper in a large skillet; drain excess fat.
2. Stir in 400 g beans, 250 ml picante sauce, and 30 g taco seasoning.
3. Cook the mixture over medium heat until all ingredients are well blended and heated through.
4. Transfer the mixture into a 12 x 8 x 2 inch baking dish.
5. Sprinkle 200 g shredded Cheddar evenly over the top.
6. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 5 minutes or until the cheese is completely melted.
7. Serve hot with 1 bag of tortilla chips.

title: Ugly Shonton Meat Balls
description: Despite their name, these meat balls are delicious! Made with ground chuck and simmered in a creamy mushroom and wine sauce, they are a comforting meal option.
instructions:
1. Finely chop the first 1 small onion and 2 cloves garlic.
2. In a large bowl, combine 500 g ground chuck, the chopped onion and garlic, the second small onion (finely diced), 1 slice white bread (crumbled), 1 egg, and 1 tsp salt.
3. Mix well and form the mixture into balls.
4. Place meat balls in a skillet and brown gently on all sides over medium heat.
5. Pour 120 ml wine over the meat balls and simmer until the liquid has evaporated.
6. In a separate bowl, whisk together 300 ml cream of mushroom soup and 150 ml milk until smooth.
7. Pour the soup mixture over the meat balls and simmer until the sauce has reduced by half.

title: Dorothy'S Old Fashioned Saltwater Dills
description: A classic recipe for old-fashioned saltwater dill pickles. Cucumbers are brined with garlic, dill, and optional hot peppers for a crisp and tangy treat.
instructions:
1. In a large pot, combine 2 l water, 500 ml cider vinegar, and 100 g salt.
2. Bring the mixture to a rolling boil.
3. Place prepared cucumbers and desired aromatics into sterilized jars.
4. Pour the boiling brine over the cucumbers in the jars until they are completely submerged.
5. Seal the jars tightly and store in a cool place for 30 days before opening.

title: Herbed Mushroom Salad
description: A refreshing and savory mushroom salad marinated in a blend of herbs, lemon, and Worcestershire sauce. Serve over a bed of shredded lettuce for a light appetizer or side dish.
instructions:
1. Clean and slice 250 g fresh mushrooms and place them in a small bowl.
2. In a separate container, whisk together 60 ml salad oil, 30 ml lemon juice, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp marjoram, 1/2 tsp basil, 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce, and 1 tsp soy sauce.
3. Pour the marinade over the mushrooms and toss to coat evenly.
4. Cover and marinate in the refrigerator for a few hours or overnight.
5. Arrange 200 g shredded lettuce into salad bowls, top with the marinated mushrooms, and serve.

title: Quickie Meat And Cheese Dip
description: An incredibly simple and fast cheese and chili dip, perfect for a quick snack. Just microwave Velveeta cheese and chili, then serve with chips.
instructions:
1. Cut the 450 g Velveeta cheese into small, uniform cubes to ensure even melting.
2. Place the cheese cubes and the 425 g brand chili without beans into a microwave-safe bowl.
3. Heat in the microwave on high for 2 minutes, then stir the mixture thoroughly.
4. Continue heating in 30-second intervals, stirring between each, until the cheese is completely melted and the dip is hot and smooth.

title: Fasta Pasta - 10 Minute Pasta Sauce
description: A quick and vibrant pasta sauce featuring fresh tomatoes, basil, garlic, and a hint of red wine. This sauce comes together in just 10 minutes, perfect for a fast meal.
instructions:
1. In a heavy skillet, heat 30 ml olive oil. Add 500 g chopped fresh tomatoes and 30 ml of the red wine.
2. Sauté over medium heat until the tomatoes cook down and become soft and tender.
3. Finely mince the 3 cloves of garlic and 1 medium yellow onion.
4. Add the minced garlic, onion, 15 g fresh basil, 400 g of processed tomatoes, and the remaining 90 ml of red wine to the skillet.
5. Stir in 5 g sugar and salt to taste.
6. Cook on high heat for 2 minutes, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes before serving.

title: Party Cucumber Sandwiches
description: Elegant and easy party cucumber sandwiches, perfect for appetizers or tea time. Cream cheese and mayonnaise are flavored with Italian dressing mix and spread on rye bread, topped with fresh cucumber slices.
instructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine 225 g cream cheese, 60 ml mayonnaise, and 15 g Italian dressing mix until smooth and well blended.
2. Spread a generous layer of the cream cheese mixture onto each slice of the 1 loaf party rye bread.
3. Peel and thinly slice 2 large cucumbers into rounds.
4. Place a cucumber slice on top of each prepared bread slice and serve immediately.

title: *Low-Cholesterol Pound Cake*
description: A moist and tender low-cholesterol pound cake, perfect for dessert or a snack. This recipe uses margarine and egg substitutes to create a lighter version of a classic.
instructions:
1. Cream 225 g corn oil margarine, 50 g shortening, and 400 g sugar in a mixer bowl until light and fluffy.
2. Add 1 egg white and beat well.
3. Add 240 ml egg substitute and beat well.
4. Gradually add 375 g flour alternately with 180 ml milk, beginning and ending with flour, beating well after each addition.
5. Stir in 1 tsp butter and 2 tsp vanilla flavorings.
6. Pour the batter into a greased and floured tube pan.
7. Bake at 350° for 1 hour and 15 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean.

title: Potato Pancake
description: A simple yet satisfying potato pancake, crispy on the outside and tender within. Potatoes are pan-fried until golden, seasoned with salt and pepper.
instructions:
1. Put 1 tablespoon olive oil and 1 tablespoon butter in a large skillet over medium heat until the butter melts.
2. Grate or slice the 1 kg potatoes and place them into the skillet.
3. Press the potatoes down into an even compact layer and season with 1 teaspoon season salt and 1/2 teaspoon pepper.
4. Cook, covered, for 5 minutes.
5. Uncover, turn the heat to low, and cook for 15 minutes more.
6. Slide the pancake onto a plate.
7. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil and 1 tablespoon butter into the skillet.
8. When the butter melts, put the pancake back into the skillet with the browned side up.
9. Press down to compact the potatoes and cook, uncovered, for 15 minutes more until crispy.
10. Cut into 4 wedges and serve warm.

title: Hamburger Barbecue(Sloppy Joes)
description: Classic Hamburger Barbecue, often known as Sloppy Joes, featuring seasoned ground beef simmered in a tangy tomato-based sauce. Perfect served on buns for a family-friendly meal.
instructions:
1. Melt 30 g margarine in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Add 500 g hamburger meat and cook until browned, breaking it into small pieces.
3. Finely chop and stir in 1 medium onion, 1 medium green pepper, and 2 stalks celery.
4. Cook the mixture over low heat for 5 minutes until vegetables are slightly softened.
5. Blend in 240 ml tomato sauce, 240 ml chili sauce, 15 ml Worcestershire sauce, salt to taste, pepper to taste, 15 g sugar, and 15 ml vinegar.
6. Simmer for about 15 minutes while stirring often, then serve on hamburger buns.

title: Heavenly Ham Salad
description: A refreshing ham salad featuring shell macaroni, crisp vegetables, and a creamy yogurt-based dressing. This dish is perfect for picnics or a light lunch.
instructions:
1. Cook 500 g shell macaroni in boiling water until tender, then drain and set aside to cool.
2. Dice 400 g ham, 3 stalks celery, 6 radishes, and 1 large cucumber into bite-sized pieces.
3. In a large bowl, combine the cooked macaroni, diced ham, celery, radishes, and cucumber.
4. In a separate small bowl, whisk together 120 ml mayonnaise, 120 ml plain yogurt, 15 ml mustard, and 5 g garlic salt until smooth.
5. Pour the dressing over the salad items and toss thoroughly to coat.
6. Serve immediately or cover and chill in the refrigerator until ready to eat.
7. Keep any leftovers refrigerated.

title: English Pasty
description: A hearty English Pasty, filled with layers of diced potatoes, minced onion, and tender pork loin, all encased in a savory pie crust. Perfect for a comforting meal.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F and place 250 g of the crust into an 8-inch pie pan to form the bottom layer.
2. Peel and dice 300 g potatoes and spread them evenly over the bottom crust.
3. Mince 1 onion and sprinkle it over the potatoes.
4. Cut 250 g pork loin into small pieces and spread them evenly on top of the vegetables.
5. Season the filling with salt to taste.
6. Cover with the remaining 250 g of crust, vent the top, and bake for 1 hour.

title: Breakfast Blintz Souffle
description: A sweet and comforting breakfast blintz souffle featuring frozen cheese blintzes baked in a rich custard. This dish is perfect for a special breakfast or brunch.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 175°C (350°F).
2. Melt 115 g butter in a 13 x 9-inch baking pan.
3. Place 12 frozen cheese blintzes in the pan and turn them to coat thoroughly with the melted butter.
4. In a separate bowl, use an electric mixer to combine 4 eggs, 475 ml sour cream, 50 g sugar, and 5 ml vanilla until smooth.
5. Pour the egg and sour cream mixture over the blintzes, ensuring they are completely covered.
6. Bake for 50 minutes until the top is golden and set.
7. Serve warm with optional toppings such as applesauce or jam.

title: American Pierogi Casserole
description: An 'Americanized' take on pierogi in casserole form, featuring layers of noodles, mashed potato and onion filling, and American cheese. It's a comforting and hearty dish suitable for families.
instructions:
1. Peel and chop 1 kg potatoes, then boil in salted water until tender, drain, and set aside.
2. Finely dice 1 large onion and sauté in a pan with 50 g butter until translucent and soft.
3. Add the sautéed onion to the drained potatoes and mash them together until smooth.
4. Boil 500 g noodles according to package directions, drain, and set aside.
5. Grease a large casserole dish and spread 250 g of the cooked noodles in an even layer at the bottom.
6. Spread the mashed potato and onion mixture over the noodles.
7. Top the potato layer with 150 g American cheese slices.
8. Add the remaining 250 g noodles over the cheese and top with the final 150 g American cheese slices.
9. Dot the top with the remaining 50 g butter and cover the dish.
10. Bake in a 350°F (175°C) oven for approximately 20 minutes or until the cheese has completely melted.

title: Pan Pierogi(This Is A Lazy Way To Make Pierogi!)
description: This is a 'lazy' way to enjoy the flavors of pierogi without all the traditional effort, using lasagna noodles filled with a creamy potato and Velveeta cheese mash. Pan-fried onions complete this comforting dish.
instructions:
1. Peel and boil 1.5 kg potatoes in salted water until tender.
2. Drain potatoes and mash thoroughly with 100 g butter, 120 ml milk, and 450 g Velveeta cheese until smooth.
3. Cook 12 lasagna noodles according to package directions, drain, and set aside.
4. Finely chop 2 large onions and sauté in the remaining 100 g butter until golden brown.
5. Grease a 9x13-inch casserole dish and place 4 cooked lasagna noodles on the bottom.
6. Spread half of the potato and cheese mixture evenly over the noodles.
7. Place 4 more noodles on top, then spread half of the sautéed onions and the remaining potato mixture.
8. Top with the final 4 noodles and spread the remaining onions and butter over the surface.
9. Bake at 165°C (325°F) for 45 minutes.
10. Remove from oven and let stand for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.

title: Polish Beet Soup
description: A classic Polish beet soup, rich in flavor with tender spareribs, bay leaf, and a delightful tang from vinegar. This vibrant red soup is a hearty and traditional dish.
instructions:
1. Separate the 1 kg dark red beets from their liquid; drain the juice into a glass and mix it with 2 tbsp vinegar and 1 tsp salt.
2. Cut the 1 kg dark red beets into thin strips and place them in a bowl with 3 tbsp vinegar and 1 tsp salt, then set aside.
3. Place 500 g spareribs in a large pot with water and 2 bay leaves, then simmer until meat is tender.
4. Add 1 large onion to the broth for flavor during the simmering process.
5. Incorporate the prepared beets and their flavored juice into the pot.
6. Finish the soup by stirring in 200 ml sour cream before serving.

title: Stella Adamek'S Rye Bread
description: Homemade rye bread combining different flours for a rich texture. It involves proofing, kneading, and baking, perfect for a hearty sandwich or alongside a warm meal.
instructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine 500 ml warm water, 20 g brown sugar, 30 g shortening, 10 g salt, and 400 g rye flour.
2. In a small cup, dissolve 14 g yeast and 5 g sugar in 60 ml water, then add this mixture to the bowl.
3. Gradually mix in 1.1 kg white flour until a dough forms.
4. Turn the dough out onto a floured board and knead for 8 to 10 minutes until smooth.
5. Place the dough in a greased container, cover, and let it rise until it has doubled in size.

title: Trish'S Polska Kielbasa Soup
description: A comforting Polish kielbasa soup, enriched with chicken broth and hearty vegetables like carrots, cabbage, and broccoli. Quick to thicken with instant mashed potatoes for a satisfying meal.
instructions:
1. In a large pot, combine 2 l chicken broth, 500 ml water, 500 g sliced Polska kielbasa, and 3 sliced carrots.
2. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for one hour.
3. Add 400 g shredded cabbage and 300 g broccoli florets to the pot.
4. Continue to simmer until vegetables are tender, approximately 15-20 minutes.
5. Gradually stir in 100 g instant mashed potatoes until the soup reaches your desired thickness.
6. Season with pepper and all-purpose seasoning to taste.

title: Stuffed Hot Peppers(Canning Recipe)
description: A canning recipe for stuffed hot peppers filled with sauerkraut and garlic, preserved in a vinegar brine. Ideal for a spicy and tangy condiment to enjoy year-round.
instructions:
1. Wearing rubber gloves, core 20 hot peppers carefully to keep them whole.
2. Wash the peppers and boil them in water for 2 minutes.
3. Remove peppers and cool them immediately in ice water.
4. Stuff each cooled pepper with 500 g sauerkraut.
5. Pack the stuffed peppers tightly into sterilized jars.
6. Add 1 sliced clove of garlic and 1/2 tsp alum to each jar.
7. Prepare a brine by mixing 1 l water, 500 ml vinegar, and 60 g salt; pour the solution over the peppers to cover them.
8. Process the jars in a hot water bath for 3 to 5 minutes, then seal tightly.

title: San Jose Quiche
description: A savory San Jose Quiche featuring ground beef, onions, garlic, and green peppers, seasoned with Mexican spices. Layered with tortillas and cheese, this is a hearty and flavorful main dish.
instructions:
1. Brown 500 g ground beef in a skillet. Add 1 chopped medium onion, 2 minced cloves garlic, and 1 diced green pepper. Cook for 5 minutes.
2. Stir in 250 ml green chili salsa, 1 tsp oregano, 0.5 tsp cumin, and 1 tbsp chili powder. Mix well.
3. Grease a casserole dish and place a layer of 200 g corn tortillas in the bottom. Spread the meat mixture evenly over the tortillas.
4. Spread 100 g diced chili Ortega over the meat and top with 200 g shredded cheese.
5. In a separate bowl, whisk 6 eggs with 250 ml milk.
6. Pour the egg mixture over the layered ingredients in the casserole dish.
7. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 25 to 30 minutes or until the quiche is set.
8. Garnish with 50 g sliced olives before serving.

title: Fried Fish Batter
description: A simple and quick batter for perfectly fried fish fillets. Using pancake mix, it creates a crispy coating in just minutes.
instructions:
1. In a medium bowl, whisk 1 egg with 125 g pancake mix to create a smooth batter.
2. Place 100 g additional pancake mix in a separate shallow dish.
3. Dredge each fish fillet in the 100 g additional pancake mix until fully coated.
4. Dip the coated fillets into the egg and pancake mix batter.
5. Fry the battered fish in hot oil for 4 to 6 minutes until golden brown.

title: Russian Cole Slaw
description: A refreshing Russian-style coleslaw with finely chopped cabbage and green onions, lightly sweetened with sugar and tangy vinegar. A simple side dish that complements any meal.
instructions:
1. Finely chop 500 g cabbage and 100 g celery into thin strips.
2. Slice 30 g green onions into small rings and combine with the cabbage and celery in a large bowl.
3. In a separate small bowl, whisk together 25 g sugar, 30 ml vinegar, 5 g mustard, 2 g salt, and 60 ml salad oil until the dressing is emulsified.
4. Pour the dressing over the vegetables and toss thoroughly to ensure even coating.

title: Campfire Goulash
description: A hearty campfire goulash featuring sweet sausage, onions, potatoes, and tomatoes. This one-pot meal is perfect for outdoor cooking or a comforting weeknight dinner, simmering to rich perfection.
instructions:
1. In a large saucepan or Dutch oven, saute 500 g sweet sausage until lightly browned on both sides.
2. Add 1 chopped large onion and saute until transparent. Add 800 g gratin potatoes and the included sauce mix.
3. Gradually stir in 500 ml water.
4. Bring the mixture to a boil and cover. Simmer for 15 minutes, stirring often.
5. Add 3 diced tomatoes and 1 sliced green pepper. Mix well.
6. Cover and simmer for 10 minutes longer before serving.

title: Stew Beef Casserole
description: A simple and comforting stew beef casserole, slow-baked with a blend of savory soups and sauces. This easy-to-prepare dish yields tender beef and a rich flavor, perfect for a cozy dinner.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 150°C (300°F).
2. Season 900 g stew beef with 5 g salt.
3. In a large bowl, combine 300 ml cream of mushroom soup, 300 ml tomato soup, 300 ml onion soup, 60 ml steak sauce, and 15 ml Worcestershire sauce, mixing until smooth.
4. Place the seasoned beef into a casserole dish and pour the sauce mixture over it, stirring to coat evenly.
5. Cover the dish tightly and bake for 3 hours until the beef is tender.
6. Serve hot over a bed of rice.

title: Challah
description: A traditional Challaha, a rich and slightly sweet bread, characterized by its beautiful braided shape. Made with yeast, eggs, and honey, it's perfect for holidays or a special breakfast.
instructions:
1. Dissolve 15 g yeast in 250 ml warm water with 2 tablespoons of the sugar; cover and let stand in a warm place until foamy.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine 50 g butter, 10 g salt, and 100 ml boiling water until the butter is melted.
3. Stir 60 ml oil, the remaining 100 g sugar, 60 ml honey, and 120 ml milk into the butter mixture.
4. Add 6 eggs to the bowl one at a time, mixing well after each addition.
5. Mix in the yeast mixture and approximately 500 g of the flour at low speed until smooth.
6. Gradually add the remaining 700 g flour, mixing well until a dough forms.
7. Turn the dough onto a floured surface and knead for 10 minutes, adding a little extra flour if needed to prevent sticking.
8. Place the dough in a buttered bowl, turn to coat the top with butter, cover, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour until doubled in size.
9. Punch the dough down to release air bubbles.
10. Cover and let the dough rise again for 1 hour until doubled before shaping and baking.

title: Swiss Potato Pancakes
description: Crispy Swiss potato pancakes, enhanced with Swiss cheese and a hint of onions. Pan-fried until golden, these savory pancakes are perfect as a side dish or light meal.
instructions:
1. To make the crust, mix 250 g flour, 1 tbsp sugar, and 0.5 tsp salt in a bowl.
2. Cut in 100 g vegetable shortening with a fork until crumbly.
3. In a separate bowl, whisk 1 egg, 3 tbsp water, and 1 tsp vinegar; stir this into the flour mixture to form a dough.
4. Divide dough into 5 portions, wrap in plastic, and chill for 30 minutes.
5. Grate 500 g potatoes and 1 large onion; squeeze out excess moisture.
6. Mix the potatoes and onion with 200 g grated Swiss cheese, 100 g cream cheese, 50 g diced red pepper, 2 eggs, 30 g flour, 1 tsp salt, and 0.5 tsp pepper.
7. Heat 30 g oleo and 15 ml salad oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
8. Roll out the chilled dough portions into circles, fill with the potato mixture, and fold or cook as patties in the hot oil until golden brown on both sides.

title: Crock-Pot Kielbasa
description: Slow-cooked kielbasa simmered in a tangy-sweet sauce of ketchup, mustard, vinegar, and brown sugar. Perfect for a cozy meal, as an appetizer, or in a bun.
instructions:
1. Place 900 g kielbasa in a pot and pour over 355 ml beer; boil for 10 minutes.
2. In a crock-pot, whisk together 240 ml ketchup, 30 g mustard, 15 ml vinegar, and 100 g brown sugar.
3. Remove the kielbasa from the beer, cut into small bite-size pieces, and add them to the crock-pot mixture.
4. Stir to coat the meat thoroughly.
5. Cover and cook on high for 2 to 3 hours or on low for 3 to 5 hours.
